###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Write not happening?

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Ron" <NoSoy@swbell.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-10T22:15:19-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a92v4c$c7v$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<jVMs8.31382$4L4.1174571@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a90795$fhf$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com> <oJNs8.31397$4L4.1187353@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a90fq3$qol$1@suaar1ac.prod.compuserve.com> <ex4t8.34396$ud6.1295775@typhoon.austin.rr.com>`

```
This does seem like a very weird error. The manual gives this description: 
"You made a keyed request for access to an entry-sequenced data set.  Or,
you issued a GETIX or PUTIX to an entry-sequenced data set or fixed-length 
RRDS." 

It sounds like you just ran the idcams delete/define. You are right that 
vsam files in that state must be handled differently. If the file has just
been defined, in COBOL you can only ONLY open the file OUTPUT (NOT I-O). 
I suspect this is what is wrong. VSAM expects that the file will be loaded with
REPRO or with a program. If that's the case the error message would kind of
make sense because no index exists as yet. Once there is data in the file you
can open it I-O and do random updates. You cannot open a file for OUTPUT if 
data is already in it UNLESS it is defined with REUSE, in which case it would
write over the file. So if you have a freshly defined file and you're opening
I-O you need to put a record in it first with File-Aid or something. I usually
put a record with all low-values in the key.  

Now a few suggestions. If you need to initialize an empty file with a lot of data,
do it in load mode (OUTPUT). Sort the data in key sequence and load the file using
repro or sort or a program. It saves tons of time. Opening I-O and doing inserts
is dog slow. But, of course if what you're testing is random updates you can't do
that. I would also specifically allocate some index space CYLINDERS (5 2). I am not
sure of the default allocation and I just feel better knowing exactly what index is
going to be.      

Good Luck,
Ron
                                                

  "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@NO-SPAM.hot.rr.com> wrote in message news:ex4t8.34396$ud6.1295775@typhoon.austin.rr.com...

  memory that you have to access empty VSAM files differently than you do a 
  file that doesn't have any records. If that a false memory, or am I really remembering

And that is pretty much exactly what the issue was. OUTPUT worked on the test file, so I deleted and 
recreated the production file, and it loaded just fine. (*sigh*) Boy, I feel pretty stupid sometimes. 
Guess it comes from switching languages and platforms 5-6 times per day. 

Thanks for the help, I really appreciate it. :) :) :) 

-Paul

  repro or sort or a program. It saves tons of time. Opening I-O and doing inserts
  is dog slow. But, of course if what you're testing is random updates you can't do
  that. I would also specifically allocate some index space CYLINDERS (5 2). I am not
  sure of the default allocation and I just feel better knowing exactly what index is
  going to be.      

  Good Luck,
  Ron
                                                  

    "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@NO-SPAM.hot.rr.com> wrote in message news:ex4t8.34396$ud6.1295775@typhoon.austin.rr.com...

Ron
  "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@NO-SPAM.hot.rr.com> wrote in message news:Bo7t8.35656$ud6.1380194@typhoon.austin.rr.com...
  And that is pretty much exactly what the issue was. OUTPUT worked on the test file, so I deleted and 
  recreated the production file, and it loaded just fine. (*sigh*) Boy, I feel pretty stupid sometimes.

  Thanks for the help, I really appreciate it. :) :) :) 

  -Paul

    "Ron" <NoSoy@swbell.net> wrote in message news:a92v4c$c7v$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com...
    This does seem like a very weird error. The manual gives this description: 
    "You made a keyed request for access to an entry-sequenced data set.  Or,
    you issued a GETIX or PUTIX to an entry-sequenced data set or fixed-length 
    RRDS." 

    It sounds like you just ran the idcams delete/define. You are right that 
    vsam files in that state must be handled differently. If the file has just
    been defined, in COBOL you can only ONLY open the file OUTPUT (NOT I-O). 
    I suspect this is what is wrong. VSAM expects that the file will be loaded with
    REPRO or with a program. If that's the case the error message would kind of
    make sense because no index exists as yet. Once there is data in the file you
    can open it I-O and do random updates. You cannot open a file for OUTPUT if 
    data is already in it UNLESS it is defined with REUSE, in which case it would
    write over the file. So if you have a freshly defined file and you're opening
    I-O you need to put a record in it first with File-Aid or something. I usually
    put a record with all low-values in the key.  

    Now a few suggestions. If you need to initialize an empty file with a lot of data,
    do it in load mode (OUTPUT). Sort the data in key sequence and load the file using
    repro or sort or a program. It saves tons of time. Opening I-O and doing inserts
    is dog slow. But, of course if what you're testing is random updates you can't do
    that. I would also specifically allocate some index space CYLINDERS (5 2). I am not
    sure of the default allocation and I just feel better knowing exactly what index is
    going to be.      

    Good Luck,
    Ron
                                                    

      "Paul Raulerson" <praulerson@NO-SPAM.hot.rr.com> wrote in message news:ex4t8.34396$ud6.1295775@typhoon.austin.rr.com...

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T11:52:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<GNet8.3672$w31.447@nwrddc01.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<jVMs8.31382$4L4.1174571@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a90795$fhf$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com> <oJNs8.31397$4L4.1187353@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a90fq3$qol$1@suaar1ac.prod.compuserve.com> <ex4t8.34396$ud6.1295775@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a92v4c$c7v$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com>`

```
Ron <NoSoy@swbell.net> wrote in message
news:a92v4c$c7v$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com...
>It sounds like you just ran the idcams delete/define. You are right that
>vsam files in that state must be handled differently. If the file has just
>been defined, in COBOL you can only ONLY open the file OUTPUT (NOT I-O).

What I do when I am working with VSAM files subject to periodic
DELETE/DEFINE (which is darn near any VSAM file from which DELETEs are done)
is OPEN the file I-O and interrogate the status. If the open fails, I open
it OUTPUT, CLOSE it, then OPEN it I-O.

If OPEN fails now, the problem is *NOT*  a 'virgin'  status and I give up.
If the OPEN succeeds the second time, all is well.

Why screw around with File-Aid or REPRO when you can handle it in your COBOL
code?

MCM
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Write not happening?

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "Ron" <NoSoy@swbell.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T12:46:42-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a94i65$39g$1@suaar1ab.prod.compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<jVMs8.31382$4L4.1174571@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a90795$fhf$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com> <oJNs8.31397$4L4.1187353@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a90fq3$qol$1@suaar1ac.prod.compuserve.com> <ex4t8.34396$ud6.1295775@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a92v4c$c7v$1@suaar1aa.prod.compuserve.com> <GNet8.3672$w31.447@nwrddc01.gnilink.net>`

```
You are right. That is a good solution in a production environment. I was
specifically referring to using File-Aid or REPRO in a one-time or testing/debugging
situation to facilitate locating the problem we were discussing.

### I NEED HELP ASAP WITH MY COBOL PROGRAM!!! PLEASE HELP ME!!!

- **From:** cmv7810@yahoo.com (CMV)
- **Date:** 2002-04-10T09:42:13-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<834c8f9f.0204100842.39f4f2d5@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello: 

I am just learning COBOL for my class and have a few questions. 

The below are the: 
1. Program Operation 
2. Report Layout 
3. Program Input 
4. The COBOL code I have written thus far 
5. Questions 

These files were provided to me by my instructor. 

Program Operation/Requirements 

1. Process each input record. 
2. Print the three heading lines on the 
first page and on each following page of 
the report. 
3. The input-record are in sequence by the 
cost center field For each record do the 
following: 
a.Print an output detail line as 
specified on the print chart. On the 
detail line, provide group indicator 
for the cost center. That is, print the 
cost center field only on the first 
detail line for that cost center. 

b.Accumulate the base monthly salary 
field. 
4. Whenever the cost center changes, print a 
control total line (one asterisk total) 
5. Accumulate the cost-center control totals 
into the report total. After all input 
is processed, print the report total line. 
6. Provide for a span of 20 lines per page. 
Do not permit control-total lines to be 
printed at the top of a new page. Line 
spacing is handled as follows: 
a.Single space between the detail lines 
with following exceptions: 
i.Double space between last heading 
line and the first detail line. 
ii.Double space between control total 
line and the first detail line of the 
next control group. 
b.Triple space the report total line from 
the preceding year control total line. 


Report layout: 

Line # 
1 
2 
3 
4 BROOKLAWN COST CENTER SUMMARY (9-5) Page zz9 
5 
6 COST DATE BASE 
7 CTR EMPLOYEE NAME HIRED SPECIALITY SALARY 
8 
9 XXX XXXXXXXXXXXXX XX/XX/XX XXXXXXXX Z,ZZZ.99 
10XXX XXXXXXXXXXXXX XX/XX/XX XXXXXXXX Z,ZZZ.99 
11 
12 
13 
14 TOTAL FOR COST CENTER XXX Z,ZZZ.99* 
15 
16 
17 REPORT TOTAL Z,ZZZ,ZZZ.99** 
18 
19 
20 


Data & Format from Input File 

3-25 Name 
26-32 Spaces 
33-46 Speciality 
47-52 Hire Date 
53-57 Spaces 
58-60 Cost Center 
61-67 Spaces 
68-73 Salary (9999v99)

John Doe bbbbbbbSurgery 010199bbbb101bbbbbbb135000 
John Smith bbbbbbbSurgery 010199bbbb101bbbbbbb110075

Linda Curtis bbbbbbbER 010199bbbb151bbbbbbb128050 
Nancy Nelson bbbbbbbPediatrics 010199bbbb201bbbbbbb128050 
Linda Moore bbbbbbbPediatrics 010199bbbb201bbbbbbb128050 
Barbara Moore bbbbbbbPediatrics 010199bbbb201bbbbbbb128050 
Paul Curtis bbbbbbbPediatrics 010199bbbb201bbbbbbb128050 

My Code...what I have so far: 
IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. 
* 
PROGRAM-ID. PROGRAM3. 
* 
ENVIRONMENT DIVISION. 
* 
INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION. 
* 
FILE-CONTROL. 
* 
SELECT EMP-FILE-INPUT 
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL 
ASSIGN TO "RECORD.TXT". 
SELECT EMP-REPORT-OUT 
ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL 
ASSIGN TO "REPORT.TXT". 
* 
DATA DIVISION. 
* 
FILE SECTION. 
* 
FD EMP-FILE-INPUT. 
RECORD CONTAINS 80 CHARACTERS. 
DATA RECORD IS EMPLOYEE-MASTER-RECORD. 
* 
01 EMPLOYEE-MASTER-RECORD. 
05 FILLER PIC X(2). 
05 EMP-NAME PIC X(23). 
05 FILLER PIC X(7). 
05 EMP-SPECIALITY PIC X(14). 
05 EMP-HIRE-DATE PIC X(6). 
05 FILLER PIC X(4). 
05 EMP-COST-CENTER PIC X(3). 
05 FILLER PIC X(7). 
05 EMP-SALARY PIC 9(4)V9(2). 
* 
FD EMP-REPORT-OUT. 
* 
01 PRINT-AREA PIC X(132). 
* 
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION. 
* 
01 SWITCHES. 
05 EMP-FILE-INPUT-EOF-SWITCH PIC X VALUE "N". 
* 
01 PRINT-FIELDS. 
05 PAGE-COUNT PIC S9(3) VALUE ZERO. 
05 LINES-ON-PAGE PIC S9(3) VALUE +20. 
05 LINE-COUNT PIC S9(3) VALUE +99. 
05 SPACE-CONTROL PIC S9. 
* 
01 HEADING-LINE-4. 
05 FILLER PIC X(29) VALUE "BROOKLAWN COST CENTER SUMMARY".

01 HEADING-LINE-6.

01 HEADING-LINE-14. 
05 FILLER PIC X(10) VALUE SPACE.

05 EMP-SALARY PIC 9(4)V9(2).

05 EMP-SALARY PIC 9(4)V9(2). 
05 FILLER PIC X(25) VALUE "TOTAL FOR COST CENTER 201". 
05 FILLER PIC X(24) VALUE SPACE. 
05 EMP-SALARY PIC 9(4)V9(2). 
* 
01 HEADING-LINE-17. 
05 FILLER PIC X(10) VALUE SPACE. 
05 FILLER PIC X(12) VALUE "REPORT TOTAL". 
05 FILLER PIC X(37) VALUE SPACE. 
05 REPORT-TOTAL PIC 9(4)V9(2). 
* 
01 EMPLOYEE-LINE. 
05 EL-NAME PIC 9(2). 
05 FILLER PIC X(6) VALUE SPACE. 
05 EL-SPECIALITY PIC X(13). 
05 EL-HIRE-DATE PIC X(5). 
05 FILLER PIC X(4) VALUE SPACE. 
05 EL-COST-CENTER PIC X(2). 
05 FILLER PIC X(6) VALUE SPACE. 
05 EL-SALARY PIC X(9999V99). 
* 
PROCEDURE DIVISION. 
* 
000-PREPARE-EMPLOYEE-REPORT. 
* 
OPEN INPUT EMP-FILE-INPUT 
OUTPUT EMP-REPORT-OUT. 
PERFORM 200-PREPARE-EMPLOYEE-LINES. 
UNTIL EMP-FILE-INPUT-EOF-SWITCH = "Y". 
PERFORM 245-COST-CENTER-TOTALS. 
PERFORM 240-CALCULATE-REPORT-TOTAL. 
PERFORM 235-PRINT-HEADING-LINES-BOTTOM. 
CLOSE EMP-FILE-INPUT 
STOP RUN. 
* 
200-PREPARE-EMPLOYEE-LINES. 
* 
PERFORM 210-READ-RECORD. 
IF EMP-FILE-INPUT-EOF-SWITCH = "N" 
PERFORM 220-PRINT-EMPLOYEE-LINE. 
* 
210-READ-EMPLOYEE-RECORD. 
* 
READ EMP-FILE-INPUT 
AT END 
MOVE "Y" TO EMP-FILE-INPUT-EOF-SWITCH. 
* 
220-PRINT-EMPLOYEE-LINE. 
* 
IF LINE-COUNT <= PAGE-COUNT 
PERFORM 230-PRINT-HEADING-LINES-TOP. 
MOVE EMP-COST-CENTER-NUMBER TO EL-COST-CENTER-NUMBER. 
MOVE EMP-NAME TO EL-EMPLOYEE-NAME. 
MOVE EMP-HIRE-DATE TO EL-DATE-HIRED. 
MOVE EMP-SPECIALITY TO EL-SPECIALITY. 
MOVE EMP-SALARY TO EL-BASE-SALARY. 
MOVE EMPLOYEE-LINE TO PRINT-AREA. 
WRITE PRINT-AREA. 
ADD 1 TO LINE-COUNT. 
MOVE 1 TO SPACE-CONTROL. 
* 
230-PRINT-HEADING-LINES-TOP. 
* 
ADD 1 TO PAGE-COUNT. 
MOVE PAGE-COUNT TO HL1-PAGE-NUMBER. 
ADD 3 TO LINE-COUNT. 
ADVANCE 3 LINES. 
ADD 1 TO LINE-COUNT. 
MOVE HEADING-LINE-4 TO PRINT-AREA. 
WRITE PRINT-AREA. 
ADD 2 TO LINE-COUNT. 
ADVANCE 2 LINES. 
MOVE HEADING-LINE-6 TO PRINT-AREA. 
WRITE PRINT-AREA. 
ADD 1 TO LINE-COUNT. 
ADVANCE 1 LINES. 
MOVE HEADING-LINE-7 TO PRINT-AREA. 
WRITE PRINT-AREA. 
MOVE 2 TO SPACE-CONTROL. 
* 
235-PRINT-HEADING-LINES-BOTTOM. 
* 
MOVE 2 TO SPACE-CONTROL. 
ADVANCE 2 LINES. 
MOVE HEADING-LINE-14 TO PRINT-AREA. 
WRITE PRINT-AREA. 
MOVE 2 TO SPACE-CONTROL. 
ADVANCE 2 LINES. 
MOVE HEADING-LINE-17 TO PRINT-AREA. 
WRITE PRINT-AREA. 
MOVE 3 TO SPACE-CONTROL. 
ADVANCE 3 LINES. 
* 
240-CALCULATE-REPORT-TOTAL 
* 
MOVE 0 TO REPORT-TOTAL. 
ADD TOTAL-COST-CENTER-107 TO REPORT-TOTAL. 
ADD TOTAL-COST-CENTER-151 TO REPORT-TOTAL. 
ADD TOTAL-COST-CENTER -201 TO REPORT-TOTAL. 
* 
245-COST-CENTER-TOTALS 
* 


Questions: 
1. I know that I print the three heading lines on the first page, but
am I also printing them on each following page of the report??? If
not, what do I need to get this part to work???

2. I am providing the group indicator for the cost center on each
detail line, but I do not know how to just print the cost center field
only on the first detail line for that cost center.

3. I am having terrible problems with accumulating the base monthly
salary...how do I go about doing this???

4. I do not know how to print an * only when the cost center control
totals change...can you help???

5. I think I have accumulated the cost-center control totals into the
report-total. I am unsure how to print them.

6. I am pretty sure my program provides for a span of 20 lines per
page. I do not know how to not permit control-total lines to be
printed at the top of a new page though.

7. I think I have the line spacing all correct, as specified in the
program operation/requirements above. Please tell me if the spacing is
wrong.

I have to hand in my program in a few hours, and I have been working
on it for over a week now. I really have put a lot of effort into
trying to get it to work. I appreciate any help that you can give me
on this as soon as possible.

Thanks! 
CMV
```

Hello

I am sorry that no one, including myself found it practical to respond
to your question within a few hours, in my case the deadline was just
too close for the amount of detailed investigation and comment
required.

I think your course tutor will have by now explained the problems with
your program, that's his job.  However, I would like to say that I
thought you expressed your question extremely well and would not like
to think that the lack of timely response would stop you asking
further questions.

For future questions it would be desirable to also include the name
and version of the COBOL compiler and the platform and operating
system being used.

Better luck next time, Robert

<a id="t-42c746bc08"></a>
## Thread: Apache,MF OCDS and Linux: CGI Program crashes with ADIS RTS 192 — 4 msgs

_Topics: [`Web, XML, modern integration`](topics.md#web)_

### Apache,MF OCDS and Linux: CGI Program crashes with ADIS RTS 192

- **From:** pepe@gmx.com (Pepe)
- **Date:** 2002-04-10T12:34:40-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7cb2e24f.0204101134.c980dc9@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi,

I tried to port a NetExpress developed CGI program to Unix.
I did this already years ago on some SCO boxes with success. 
But now with Linux the program crashes constantly with 

Execution error : file 'ADIS'
error code: 192, pc=0, call=5, seg=0
192     Required terminal capability description 

I had the same problem with the SCO box. 
The solution then was to add the environment variable 
TERMINFO=/usr/lib/terminfo
to the shellscript, that started the Cobol program.

The Apache server sets the TERM Variable to 'dumb'. 
There is an entry for 'dumb' in /usr/lib/terminfo. 
I tried it with different settings of TERM. 
Also setting COBTERMINFO gave no result.

I tried it for several hours today - I'm slowly going nuts
Did i miss some RTFM?

Some facts:

Apache 1.3.20
SuSE 7.3
Micro Focus OCDS 4.1.30

TIA,

Pepe
```

#### ↳ Re: Apache,MF OCDS and Linux: CGI Program crashes with ADIS RTS 192

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T10:45:05+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB55B21.880C16A2@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<7cb2e24f.0204101134.c980dc9@posting.google.com>`

```
Pepe wrote:
> 
> I tried to port a NetExpress developed CGI program to Unix.
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> 192     Required terminal capability description

Why is your code doing _anything_ which requires ADIS ?  ANSI
ACCEPT/DISPLAY (ie UPON CONSOLE) should not need access to anything that
is in ADIS.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Apache,MF OCDS and Linux: CGI Program crashes with ADIS RTS 192

- **From:** "Russell Styles" <RWSTYLES@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T04:51:11+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```
    This sounds like one of those "You aren't supposed
to do it, but we will let you get away with it" things.

    The only option allowed on a display
verb is "with no advancing".  Just about anything
else (other than literal or data name) will
use ADIS.


"Richard Plinston" <riplin@Azonic.co.nz> wrote in message
news:3CB55B21.880C16A2@Azonic.co.nz...

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Apache,MF OCDS and Linux: CGI Program crashes with ADIS RTS 192

- **From:** pepe@gmx.com (Pepe)
- **Date:** 2002-04-10T23:37:09-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<7cb2e24f.0204102237.717fa463@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<7cb2e24f.0204101134.c980dc9@posting.google.com> <3CB55B21.880C16A2@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
Richard,

The program is 'NetExpress Generated'. There are no ANSI ACCEPT
statements in the source.
The only two accepts are 'ACCEPT FROM htmlform' and ACCEPT FROM
ENVIRONMENT-NAME. Same with DISPLAY.
MF uses  accept/display syntax to get the parameters from the server.
For this accept to work, i had to link the ACCCGI.o and two other
modules
into rts32. 
I guess, the call into ADIS happens during the initialization of the
run-time, as the error happens at PC 0,
If thats right, then i need to get rid of ADIS in the run-time?
But I don't know how.

Peter

I need to print a report in landscape mode because it is 132 chr$ wide. I
cant figure out how to tell the printer to do this. I was looking in the
help area and found some code but I do not seem to be entering it correctly.

my code......

       100-MAIN-ROUTINE.
           OPEN INPUT EMP-DATA-IN
                OUTPUT PRINT-FILE
                CALL "PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT" LPT1 BTHIRD.DET BY VALUE 1
           PERFORM 200-INITILIZATION-RTN
           PERFORM 500-PRINT-COST-REPORT-HEADER


what I found .......
****************************************************************************
**************
Opens a printer channel, giving it a document name and displaying the
printer-options dialog box in the Windows environment.

Syntax:


CALL "PC_WIN_OPEN_PRINTER_EXT" USING printer-handle

doc-name

BY VALUE options SIZE 2

RETURNING status-code

Parameters:


printer-handle PIC XX COMP-5.
doc-name PIC X(m).
options numeric literal.
status-code See status-code.

Parameters on Entry:


doc-name The document name given to the spooled job.  The name must be
null-terminated.

options A switch to select the printer options dialog box:

0 No extended options
 1 Show printer options dialog, enabling user to setup printer.
****************************************************************************
**************
any help would be appreciated. This stuff is sooooo frustrating.

Thanks in advance

PamelaRose

Well not quite - Yet <G>.

Exactly which compiler are you using. Can't be Net Express because the alpha
listing I see has only :-

PC_WIN_CHAR_TO_OEM
PC_WIN_INIT
PC_WIN_OEM_TO_CHAR

All current printer routines are referred to as :-

PC_PRINT_xxxx, or
PC_PRINTER_xxx

Thought you might be using the DOS Compiler V 3.1, but from the alpha listing,
(I'm going from the books, don't have the software loaded),  that only has :-

PC_WIN_ABOUT
PC_WIN_HANDLE

Without being able to tie it down,  suggestions :-

(1) Perhaps that "LPT1" above should read "LPT1:" (COLON after the 'one')
(2) try assigning the printer to a (disk) filename rather than a device
(3) Your earlier message said :-
      OPEN OUTPUT PRINT-FILE
       CALL "PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT" LPT1 BTHIRD.DET BY VALUE 1

    Exactly how did you specify the file entry : -

    select line-printer
    assign to filename/or LPT1:
    etc.......

    BUT...... if you are using PC_WIN...etc.... calls, they are CONTROLLING the
printer and you shouldn't have a file reference for the printer as well !

Sorry, I can't be more helpful. Any Micro Focus lurkers from California, Quebec
or Newbury looking in ? Please help the lady.

Jimmy, Calgary AB

>  my  new code......
>

> >

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Microfocus sending printer commands

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** graham@olympic.co.uk (graham)
- **Date:** 2002-04-17T13:09:29-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8a02b0a1.0204171209.238569e1@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<y_7t8.43$2c.41484@newshog.newsread.com> <lvot8.108$2c.99375@newshog.newsread.com> <3CB64F57.86506EB@shaw.ca> <5Ttt8.133$2c.125934@newshog.newsread.com>`

```
Hi,

I've not used the COBOL 3.1. PC_WIN... routines but am familiar with
the similar NetExpress PC_PRINT... I've had a quick look at the
specification of the routines (we do have the 3.1. compiler) and there
is certainly an error in your WorkingStorage ...


"PamelaRose" <prose@splatzone.com> wrote in message news:<5Ttt8.133$2c.125934@newshog.newsread.com>...
> then a
> .
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>       *

the spec. has 
             printer-handle           pic x(2) comp-5

it is the handle of the printer that has been opened, i.e. an output
parameter and you wouldn't set it (other than perhaps initialise to
zero) prior to the call. In addition the "options" parameter is a bit
flag: bit 0 selects a printer dialog box; 1 a font dialog box; 2
portrait; and 3 landscape with the proviso that you cannot use the
orientation bits (2,3) if you set bit 0. This latter is probably
logical because I suspect that if you select the printer dialog then
the printer dialog will offer an orientation selection (somewhere).

You also seem to be confusing the use of the PC_WIN routines with the
standard FILE SELECT/ASSIGN and WRITE. When using the PC_WIN routines
you do not need the FILE SELECT etc.; the PC_WIN routines deal with
all accesses to the output device.

You would use PC_WIN_PRINTER_EXT to open the printer, returning the
printer-handle and then PC_WIN_WRITE_PRINTER to send a record of data
to the printer, providing the printer-handle (and NOT the COBOL
"WRITE") and finally PC_WIN_CLOSE_PRINTER. Actually you may also need
PC_WIN_PRINTER_CONTROL to do line feeds form feeds etc. after each
record (PC_WIN_WRITE...)

hope this helps...

graham


> 
> and finially the subroutine........
…[57 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

```
Pamela,

If I read your original post you are asking how can I use the
PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT library call to print a file in landscape mode.

If this is still your question what I would do in your program is
generate your output file as a physical file and then use the call. 
The PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT will send your file to the printer.

The syntax should be as follows;

call "PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT"
     using by reference fi-file-name,
           by reference "Name of my document" & x"0",
           by value X size 2

Where fi-filename is a pic x(n) data item that will contain the name
of your file
Where X is the options that you want to use.  X can have the following
values;

Set of bits to set printer options:

Bit Decimal description
3   8       Print in landscape orientation
2   4       Print in portrait orientation
1   2       Display font dialog box
0   1       Display a printer control dialog box

Bits 2 and 3 cannot be used with bit 0 set.

So in your case X should have a value of 8, to print in landscape
mode.

Hope this helps.
```

<a id="t-33f9b0dc3c"></a>
## Thread: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"? — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Off-topic and spam`](topics.md#spam)_

### Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T09:50:32+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3cb55438_2@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<a7o5d1$4jga$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <zu6o8.102194$2q2.8621038@bin4.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <n49o8.41046$J53.1272791@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a7t6kk$3dkq$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <3cab0062_4@Usenet.com> <a91se9$6g14$1@mercury.vcu.edu>`

```
Sadly, some companies place a lot of emphasis on phone interviews as a method of "filtering" prospects.

Don't take it to heart. (If it's any consolation... I worked with CICS, almost from the time it was invented,  up until around 1992 and I don't remember what SCT means either. The EIBCALEN question is easily missed if you are nervous and they have already mentioned "zero" to throw you...and the subtle details of LINK, XCTL and START are also easily confused under the same circumstances. This is why phone interviewing is not a good practice; different individuals react differently on the phone.)

Of course, all they are trying to do is filter out people so they don't really care whether the test is "fair" or not.

Write it down to experience. After a few such interviews you will be less nervous and perform better.

Keep trying to get a face-to-face interview.

Pete.


  <bgwillia@vcu.edu> wrote in message news:a91se9$6g14$1@mercury.vcu.edu...

  Hi all, 

  Give you a quick update:  I screwed up the technical interview made over the phone on my first job opportunity.  I thought I knew my CICS down pat since I coded with it from '88 to '96 when the department began switching to Oracle and MS-Access. 

  The first question "what is meant by SCT?" threw off my game plan because I don't remember that acronym in relation with CICS coding.  Then it went downhill from there: 

  "what does it mean then EIBCALEN is zero" (I said normal condition return, actually it is the length of the COMMAREA when the CICS program is first invoked), 

  "what is the difference between XCTL and START (I said XCTL is transfering control to another program without any return abilities and START is the same thing but with return abilites, actually LINK uses a return back to calling program on a higher level and START acts like a CALL statement) ...   

  Well you get the idea.  Also messed up on JCL IDCAMS statements.  But did failly well on my COBOL - just messed up saying "PIC S9(5) COMP" is three bytes when it's four. ("PIC S9(4) COMP" is two bytes on a fullword boundary). 

  I noted down the questions and my responses and look them up to confirm.  Boy, I must of really looked red-faced when I saw how bad I did!  Made a good dent on the desk after banging my head against it for an hour saying "why! why! why!"  (just kidding about the head-banging part).  But it does y'all an idea what the recruiters are asking. 

  Now I'm getting another interview about an Oracle position at the same company.  So, now it's boning up on the vi editor and UNIX commands (haah, finger managling - let me count the ways). 

  Wish me luck, 
  Boyce Williams
```

#### ↳ Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

- **Date:** 2002-04-13T06:24:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB7CF11.5F925F48@dontspam.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<a7o5d1$4jga$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <zu6o8.102194$2q2.8621038@bin4.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <n49o8.41046$J53.1272791@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <a7t6kk$3dkq$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <3cab0062_4@Usenet.com> <a91se9$6g14$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <3cb55438_2@Usenet.com>`

```
<bgwillia@vcu.edu> wrote in message
news:a91se9$6g14$1@mercury.vcu.edu...

     Hi all, 

     Give you a quick update:  I screwed up the technical
     interview made over the phone on my first job opportunity.
      I thought I knew my CICS down pat since I coded with it
     from '88 to '96 when the department began switching to
     Oracle and MS-Access. 

     The first question "what is meant by SCT?" threw off my
     game plan because I don't remember that acronym in
     relation with CICS coding.  Then it went downhill from
     there: 

I've been working with CICS since '72 and am still using it. I consider
myself pretty knowledgeable, but I don't recognize "SCT". "System
Control Table" or "Security Control Table" come to mind, but I've never
heard of either of them. The IBM online system I worked with before CICS
did have SCT's. 

     "what does it mean then EIBCALEN is zero" (I said normal
     condition return, actually it is the length of the
     COMMAREA when the CICS program is first invoked), 

What it means when EIBCALEN = 0 is that you have no COMMAREA.

     "what is the difference between XCTL and START (I said
     XCTL is transfering control to another program without any
     return abilities and START is the same thing but with
     return abilites, actually LINK uses a return back to
     calling program on a higher level and START acts like a
     CALL statement) ...   

START invokes a separate task, whereas XCTL, LINK & CALL all invoke
programs within the existing task. In my experience, the idea of a
separate task is what people are usually interested in.

     Well you get the idea.  Also messed up on JCL IDCAMS
     statements.  But did failly well on my COBOL - just messed
     up saying "PIC S9(5) COMP" is three bytes when it's four.
     ("PIC S9(4) COMP" is two bytes on a fullword boundary). 

Not really, if you don't specify "SYNC", COMP (or BINARY) fields are
byte aligned. With "SYNC", Pic S9999 is halfword boundary aligned
(address is a multiple of 2) and S9(8)/S9(9) are fullword boundary
aligned (address is a multiple of 4).

     I noted down the questions and my responses and look them
     up to confirm.  Boy, I must of really looked red-faced
     when I saw how bad I did!  Made a good dent on the desk
     after banging my head against it for an hour saying "why!
     why! why!"  (just kidding about the head-banging part).
      But it does y'all an idea what the recruiters are asking. 

     Now I'm getting another interview about an Oracle position
     at the same company.  So, now it's boning up on the vi
     editor and UNIX commands (haah, finger managling - let me
     count the ways). 

Knock 'em dead, Boyce. 

In general, what's the market like these days (I may have more than an
academic interest in it soon), especially the NYC area?
```

```
Hy,

I think many of you might be familiar with microfocus screens.
Well I should make a box from which you can select one of the options on the
screen.
Is there anyone who can get me an example code, or where I can find a very
specific tutorial for these kind of boxes.
Also, I'm trying to find how to make a scroll box

hope someone can help me
greets,
  tomba
```

#### ↳ Re: micrfocus screens

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T02:38:08+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB647F7.2133EDB9@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<3cb55ab5$0$6964$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```


Tomba wrote:

> Hy,
>
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Which compiler are you using. I'm sending you separately some nine Micro Focus
demo programs that came with the DOS Compiler V 3.1 - should put you on the
right path, and in a DOS environment, should be compatible with all current M/F
compilers.

Regarding you comment about making a scroll box - FORGET IT ! You are obviously
much too new to COBOL. It isn't just the scrolling, but also having a handle on
file techniques, how big your scroll list is, how do you move forwards and
backwards through the list  etc...It can be done, but you would find it quite
daunting. I've even done Pop Ups and Menu Bars (yearrrrs agooooo.........).

By all means use Screen Section as a simple way to get a handle visually on what
you are programming. Any time spent on getting sophisticated with Screen Section
would be better devoted to learning calls to APIs, GUI classes or other COBOL
Windows display tools, or even Java or Visual Basic..

The zipfile will soon be on its way.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

>                RECORD KEY IS IN-Property-ID-X
 . . .
>        01 Property-Record.
>            05  IN-Property-ID-X.
>                 10  IN-Property-ID            PIC 9(5).
>            05  . . .

#### ↳ Re: Trouble with OPEN I-O

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@invalid.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T22:19:27+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<PZnt8.3337$GS6.260832@bin3.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<BKgt8.20792$vc3.77086@news-server.bigpond.net.au>`

```

"Bawdo2001" <gomi50@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:BKgt8.20792$vc3.77086@news-server.bigpond.net.au...
> Hello all,
>  Can someone help with a question. I have tried to figure this one out bby
> myself to no avail.
>
> When I try to compile I end up with the following error (MicroFocus
Animator
> Ver.2):
>
> 1035E- Key is not wholly contained within minimum record length
>
> The program is supposed to open/create a file edit it the close it. The
file
> is to be indexed. Here are some details:
>
…[28 more quoted lines elided]…
> Any help would be appreciated. Regards, Bawdo2001.

Some (many, most) compilers freak when attempting to open a file as I-O when
the file does not exist.

Try:

   OPEN OUTPUT PROPERTY-FILE.
   CLOSE PROPERTY-FILE.
   OPEN I-O PROPERTY-FILE.
   etc.

Or, better:

   OPEN I-O PROPERTY-FILE.
   IF PROPERTY-FILE-STATUS NOT = '00'
      PERFORM FILE-NOT-FOUND.

  FILE-NOT-FOUND.
      (in addition to error messages, might include)
      OPEN OUTPUT PROPERTY-FILE
      CLOSE PROPERTY-FILE
      OPEN I-O PROPERTY-FILE.


P.S.
Lose the LABEL RECORDS... clause. It's ignored.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble with OPEN I-O

- **From:** jnjsle@aol.com (Jnjsle)
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T03:27:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020411232753.28297.00003487@mb-ms.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<PZnt8.3337$GS6.260832@bin3.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`

```
Hi Jerry,

I don't think that's the problem. It seems he's getting a compile error; what
you mentioned is a runtime event.

Somehow, the compiler thinks that the record length is less than the key
length, so it sounds like it may be a rec len problem. 

Bawdo,

Just some WAGs:

Do you have a 2nd 01 record descr for that file?
Do you define all the required divs, sects and pgraphs in the required order? 
As Jerry said, the LABEL clause is not used, but if it’s coded, it should be
in margin B (not sure if it matters).
  
Also, what does this mean?
PROCEDURE DIVISION.   <=== The error is highlighted on this line.

Regards, Jack.
```

#### ↳ Re: Reading an Excel file

- **From:** cesullivan@aol.comsf (Cathy Sullivan)
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T02:36:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020411223655.24750.00000505@mb-cf.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<a94age$a9u$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`

```
In article <a94age$a9u$1@peabody.colorado.edu>, "Howard Brazee"
<howard.brazee@cusys.edu> writes:

>I need someone to FTP an excel file to the mainframe and I would like to
>avoid having to look for tabs and such to break up the data.  Is there a way
>to get this into a fixed format file for my CoBOL program to read?
>

I had to do this once.  I imported the file into Access.  That program can
write it as a file with fixed lenth records.  Be warned though, any calculated
numbers will have many decimal points, not just the nice format you see.  Maybe
Access could help with that.  I just wrote a COBOL routine to round the number.
 I created an array of characters and unstrung the number using the decimal
point into the array.  Then I calculated the value based on the position of the
character.  Or something like that.

Cathy
```

#### ↳ Re: Reading an Excel file

- **From:** "BILL" <indreboe@worldnet.att.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T04:51:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<NJtt8.210266$Yv2.67709@rwcrnsc54>`
- **References:** `<a94age$a9u$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`

```
You need to save as a "formatted text, space delimited" file type which will
generate a ".prn" file.

But first make sure you 'fix' the fields first.
Go to each column and highlight it, then go to Format, Column and Width and
make the column a fixed width
next, go to Format, Cells, and alignment to make the field right adjusted or
left adjusted as needed (alpha or numeric data)

do this for each column then go to File, Save-as, choose 'formatted text,
space delimited' and you have a decent sequential file

any problems, email me directly at    indreboe at   ATT dot Net


Bill




"Howard Brazee" <howard.brazee@cusys.edu> wrote in message
news:a94age$a9u$1@peabody.colorado.edu...
> I need someone to FTP an excel file to the mainframe and I would like to
> avoid having to look for tabs and such to break up the data.  Is there a
way
> to get this into a fixed format file for my CoBOL program to read?
```

## Thread: Child Process — 1 this month / 4 total

_Thread started [2002-03](2002-03.md#t-63f4a381b6)._

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Child Process

- **From:** "Clark F. Morris, Jr." <cfmtech@istar.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T23:48:52+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB61233.3C87AEAB@istar.ca>`
- **References:** `<3c8e31a7$0$272$ba624c82@nntp02.dk.telia.net> <3c8e895d_1@Usenet.com>`

```
Both Micro-Focus and IBM have Re-entrant as one of their compile
options.  The saving of registers has little to do with it since
mainframe COBOL always followed standard linkage conventions for that
platform.  It's just that IBM COBOL before the ANSI 85 COBOLs treated
the Working Storage and internal work areas as a part of the load module
rather than acquiring the areas separately upon entry.     

"Peter E. C. Dashwood" wrote:
> 
> Lars,
…[44 more quoted lines elided]…
>                 http://www.usenet.com
```

## Thread: relinking cobol run time system — 2 msgs

### relinking cobol run time system

- **From:** "Samir Badaoui" <sbadaoui@videotron.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T22:35:18-03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<SMrt8.12537$jD4.473054@weber.videotron.net>`

```
Hi all,
I am a DB2 DBA and I need some help about cobol word. My problem is :
In our companie we have a rts that is linked to some C programs and I want
to link this rts to a set of DB2 libraries so that I can use the embeded SQL
with cobol.
My question is how can I do that ? and if possible without recreating the

<a id="t-eb7f2b6107"></a>
## Thread: Microfocus Cobol, Optimization? — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Microfocus Cobol, Optimization?

- **From:** j6vflbl6vy6g8o001@sneakemail.com (Kevin Arthur)
- **Date:** 2002-04-11T19:50:01-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<e87297f1.0204111850.57f0443@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello...

I am hoping for some input from any of you gracious readers who might
have some insight on optimizing Microfocus Cobol programs.  General
ideas and suggestions welcome, as well as specific details on use of
Microfocus tools (like "Analyze") and/or compiler directives (such as
"PROFILE").

Have searched groups and the web, but didn't find any threads that
were particularly helpful.  Please forgive if I just missed one.
...

Writing a conversion program in Microfocus Cobol 4.0.38, target
platform Windows 2000 workstation, reading files local or on network.

Basically reading an input file, doing a bunch of processing
(including table lookups and some parsing), and spitting out a new
output file.  Approximately 4.2 million input records (~2.1 GB).

Trying to optimize performance.

Of course, I do all the basic rejections I can up front based upon
business rules. (e.g. reject records with certain fields blank, or
that represent obsolete information.)

Successively work my way up to the more complex conditions until
determined that record accepted, and then truly start processing it.

Approximately 2.8 million records are accepted for processing.

The program doesn't run too bad, but I would like it to run better.
Depending on workstation it's run on and location of input/output
files (network or local drive), it takes about 3 hours to run on
average.

Of the eight internal tables used for lookups, one of them is huge
(64K rows).
I changed it to do a binary lookup (sort it first, of course).  Not a
noticable difference much to my surprise.  But, this table (and the
other two biggies) are searched only for around 390,000 of the 2.8
million accepted input records.

So, if THAT wasn't slowing the program down, where's it spending it's
time?

(Yeah, I need to go back and verify that the search is really doing
what it's supposed to be doing.  But, questions below are still valid,
nonetheless...)

In an attempt to answer that question, I tried to use the "Analyzer",
but we seem to be missing some components.  We supposedly have
workbench, but no "wb.exe".  I did get program to compile with
"GNTANLZ" compiler directive, and attempted to "run" the resultant
.gnt file, but got a 173 on a "gntanlz" module, which we don't seem to
have either.  Oh, well.

I may try going back a compiler version to 3.2.43 where we have a
"gntanlz.lbr".  Maybe that's what it wants.

Anyway, next I tried the "PROFILE" compiler directive which is
described thus:

"Includes code in your program to produce detailed performance
statistics each time you run the program."

That worked.  I ran the program.  Expected a report file or something.
 Instead what I got was a tiny, 1600 byte file with an ".ipf"
extension.  Contents not readable by an editor, and cannot find any
trace of what to do with this file nor how to process it anywhere! 
(Not MF documentation, nor MF/Merant knowledgebase, etc.)  ***HELP!***
 Any suggestions on this?

Anyway, since I know you're probably wondering, back to basics. 
Program:

- Loads 8 internal tables for look-up's.  Range from 55 records to
64K.
  These are used to "translate" values from old system to new.
  Largest three are 5,000, 10,000, and 64,000.
  Standard serial searches, except for largest which is "search all".

- Reads ascii, fixed rec length input file.  (4.2 million input recs)

- Reads three other MF indexed files.  Two are a direct read, the
other is a "start" followed by "read nexts" until control-break.

- Does some fairly tightly coded parsing on two of the input fields on
each record.  (Phone number field and an electronic serial # field)

- No huge, ungainly, complicated nested conditions.  I try to keep the
"IF" statements fairly simple and straightword and of limited depths. 
Just a lot of 'em.

- Spits out new record containing converted values, plus a variable
number of additional, secondary records based on associated
information read from that third index file.

- Also writes a summary, a detail, and an additional index file.

- Loops back for next input record.  Repeat until done.

That's it!

In advance, thanks for any input you can provide!

Kevin Arthur
```

#### ↳ Re: Microfocus Cobol, Optimization?

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T03:55:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB65A08.3FFF7FFC@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<e87297f1.0204111850.57f0443@posting.google.com>`

```


Kevin Arthur wrote:

> Hello...
>
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>

<snip>

Can't specifically offer any solutions, but if you use some obvious (to
you <G>) keywords against your Help Files, Micro Focus certainly addresses
optimization under several subject matter headings, plus I believe, some
DIRECTIVES to offer enhancements.

The only conversion I have ever done is to convert RM/COBOL V2 data to
M/F. But volumes were an absolute flea bite, compared to what you are
talking about.

It might just be your approach. Example, back to when Noah was hewing wood
for the Ark. 1965 - Unigate (dairy company), we were using an ICT (ICL)
1500 with RCA's random access MCF(Magnetic Card File). Input was on paper
tape and data was stored on mag tape. We only used the MCF unit for "ISAM"
lookups - it was too *new" to trust it wouldn't lose our transaction data
!

Volumes, I've absolutely forgotten, but as a rough daily figure :-

- say 60 distribution depots - some 200 deliveries a day     = 12,000
- 150 van salesmen - about 50 deliveries per day                =   7,500

Two things we needed to do (1) ISAM lookup for Product Code and (2) lookup
for Customer A/c #.

The input mag tapes were sorted TWICE - Product sequence, then Customer
A/c # sequence.

(Later of course a sort had to put the data back into Customer A/c #
followed by Product #'s for the delivery document - then we printed
invoices).

Those two sorts offered the following advantages (1) Our Product File was
wrapped on one magnetic card that continuously spun around the read-write
head. So look-ups were ascending, and if IN-Product # = Previous-Product #
- use same info without a further ISAM search. Same goes for the Customer
file, although that was on some 20 cards, but nevertheless, each card was
pulled in ascending sequence. (Again if IN-A/c # (F.W. Woolworth) =
Previous-A/c # (F.W.Woolworth) - no search).

Archaic, compared to what you can do today. But given that you say your
Search Tables are sorted, might you benefit from the input going through a
couple of sorts, so that you access the tables strictly ascending - or
perhaps even use a pointer like an SQL Cursor ? Might not make the
slightest bit of difference, e.g. any sorts might eat up any time you were
trying to save - just mention it as a possible approach.

Now there's a thought - could you put those look-up tables in a DB - might
just give you the edge.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: Microfocus Cobol, Optimization?

- **From:** "Russell Styles" <RWSTYLES@worldnet.att.net>

all files are opened exclusive, NOT shared.  Copy ALL files to unshared
portion
of local drive.

    I suspect that the 3 indexed files you mentioned are the big hold up.
Since you already have several tables, I assume that they are too big to
load into a table.

Is it possible to do some sorts, and read at least some
of these things sequentially?

Check out some of the various tuneables.  One of them controls
indexed file cacheing, for indexes and data.

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<e87297f1.0204111850.57f0443@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <e87297f1.0204111850.57f0443@posting.google.com>,
Kevin Arthur <j6vflbl6vy6g8o001@sneakemail.com> wrote:
>Hello...
>
>I am hoping for some input from any of you gracious readers who might
>have some insight on optimizing Microfocus Cobol programs.

Well, one doesn't always get what one hopes for... instead, in this case, 
you get a response from me.

[snippette]

>Basically reading an input file, doing a bunch of processing
>(including table lookups and some parsing), and spitting out a new
>output file.  Approximately 4.2 million input records (~2.1 GB).

Good analysis, so far... always start with transaction volume.  What's 
the run schedule/how often do you plan on doing this?

[mas snippado]

>The program doesn't run too bad, but I would like it to run better.

I hate to ask but... for what reason(s)?  Because it needs to fit into a 
tight batch window or because a 3+ hr run offends your e'er-so-delicate 
sense of aesthetics?

Need vs. desire and all that.

>Depending on workstation it's run on and location of input/output
>files (network or local drive), it takes about 3 hours to run on
>average.

Hardware optimisation first... do your best to run it on a local machine 
with two drives... no, make that three drives, one for input file, one 
for reference files, one for output file.

(not the cheapest configuration for hardware, true, but again... need vs. 
desire)

>
>Of the eight internal tables used for lookups, one of them is huge
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>million accepted input records.

One of the sorts you might want to do is on looked-up fields, both table 
and file.  When you READ a rec you can then check

IF CURRENT-LOOKUP-FLD = PREV-LOOKUP-FLD
    PERFORM FOUND-THISUN-ALREADY THRU FTA-EX.

... or a reasonable facsimile thereof.

>
>So, if THAT wasn't slowing the program down, where's it spending it's
>time?

Someplace else?  Remember what was mentioned about changing the hardware 
configuration... and relate that to the Anciente Concepte that most 
programs are not processor-bound but hardware-bound.

(I see some wrinkled, withered lips parting into grins which come close to 
cracking some leathery, aged cheeks... 'Durned whippersnappers!  Ain't 
nobody teach these kids about hardware-bound no more?!?')

>
>(Yeah, I need to go back and verify that the search is really doing
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>but we seem to be missing some components.

Ogg the Caveman says 'Sometime good analyser between ears'.

[molto snippimente]

>- Loads 8 internal tables for look-up's.  Range from 55 records to
>64K.
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
>That's it!

Try the approach of separate disks... if you can't get a local machine 
with three then get a local with two and config with your input on the 
network drive and your reference and output files local.

(This is because output and indexed-file access take more system overhead 
than flatfile reads... again, Anciente Wisdome, up there with 'Thou Shalt 
Not Compile To Thy Source-Pack'.)

DD
```

#### ↳ Re: Microfocus Cobol, Optimization?

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@invalid.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T23:11:28+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<AQJt8.17295$043.1319037@bin6.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<e87297f1.0204111850.57f0443@posting.google.com>`

```

"Kevin Arthur" <j6vflbl6vy6g8o001@sneakemail.com> wrote in message
news:e87297f1.0204111850.57f0443@posting.google.com...
> Hello...
>
…[41 more quoted lines elided]…
> time?

-----
We'll stop here. I betch dollars-to-donuts virtually all your overhead is
spent in I-O. You would be AMAZED at how much work a PC can do in the time
it takes to fetch a single record.

So, then, speed up the I-O.

How?

Well, you already tried one trick: move all the files to the local machine
before processing then move them back when done. That way, each record moves
over the network only twice.

Someone else suggested splitting the files between several disks. That's a
good idea. Another is a second disk and a RAID controller. Another idea is
getting faster disks. Another is changing the cluster size on the disk you
have (512byte vs 4K clusters). Implement 'Dirty Writes' in the OS (if you're
not familiar with the term, check it out). If much of the work is
sequential, implement the "Read Ahead" feature of the disk drive or OS.

You might see a suggestion about not fetching a record if you already have
it; that's probably not necessary inasmuch as operating system sorts that
out for you - that is, if you want a record already in the OS's cache, the
OS won't do a physical read.

The EASY way to find out if fine-tuning the code will help is to run the
program on another machine that's twice as fast as the production machine.
Does that help 10% (the program is I-O bound) or does it help 50% (the
program is compute-bound).

Anyway, for that much data, my money is on I-O bound.






>
> (Yeah, I need to go back and verify that the search is really doing
…[59 more quoted lines elided]…
> Kevin Arthur
```

## Thread: ASCII to EBCIDIC to COMP — 2 msgs

### ASCII to EBCIDIC to COMP

- **From:** Luke
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T16:06:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3cb702cb.10659627@news.newsguy.com>`

```
Hello.

I have an ASCII text string that I am sending via MQ Series to an
OS390 COBOL program.  The MQ Bridge will perform an ASCII to EBCIDIC
conversion before passing the string as an input to a COBOL program.

My problem is thus:

I need to pass "0689" to the following COBOL field:

05  IN-BUFFER-SIZE    PIC 9(4) COMP.

DATA ELEMENT   BEG END LEN  TYPE  FORMAT
IN-BUFFER-SIZE    1        2       2        C           (4)


I have two ASCII positions to store a representation of a 4 digit
number in COBOL.  What ASCII characters do I store in the two digit
field so that it will represent 0689 once it gets into the COBOL
program?

Any help would be appreciated.  Email or posted replies welcome.  
Thanks.

Luke
ldomet@yahoo.com
```

#### ↳ Re: ASCII to EBCIDIC to COMP

- **From:** Doug Scott <dwscott@ieee.org>
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T21:41:17+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<VA.000001b8.0fc111e5@ieee.org>`
- **References:** `<3cb702cb.10659627@news.newsguy.com>`

```
> I need to pass "0689" to the following COBOL field:
> 
> 05  IN-BUFFER-SIZE    PIC 9(4) COMP.

Why? Presumably the buffer size is 6*256 + 8 * 16 + 9 (1673) bytes long, 
is that it? At a guess, you can't do it if that routine insists on 
converting every byte from ASCII to EBCDIC. Does it?

It's a byte string. Why are you sending binary data? Why not send it in 
ASCII numeric?

---

Doug

dwscott@ieee.org
```

<Lucas> wrote in message news:3cb70645.11549647@news.newsguy.com...
> Hello.
>
…[20 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks.

Well, 689 in hex is 02 B1. 02 should translate directly (STX in both), but
B1(x) is a super-script "1" in EBCDIC for which there is no ASCII
equivalent.
```

> For anyone interested in using "built-in" features of the "latest and
> greatest" IBM mainframe COBOL compiler to convert ASCII to EBCDIC *and* vice
…[35 more quoted lines elided]…
> 
COMP is COMP is COMP.  It is all binary 2 bytes, 4 bytes or 8 bytes.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: ASCII to EBCIDIC to COBOL COMP field

- **From:** jacksleight@hotmail.com (Jack Sleight)
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T08:27:15-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8a2d426e.0204160727.16b22b66@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3cb70645.11549647@news.newsguy.com> <a9adte$r9v$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net> <B8DFF611.5816%jreinhart1@cox.net>`

```
Hi Luke,

If I read you correctly, you want to move the string '0689' to the COMP field
IN-BUFFER-SIZE.

Here are 2 possible approaches:

MOVE X'0689' TO IN-BUFFER-SIZE(1:2)  This I haven't tried, but should work.
                                                             When a field is
reference modified, it's
                                                             treated as
alphanumeric, regardless of
                                                             its original class
(in this case, numeric).


This one I've used before and know it works:
 
05  IN-BUFFER-SIZE    PIC 9(4) COMP.
05  IN-BUFFER-SIZE-X REDEFINES   
    IN-BUFFER-SIZE    PIC X(2).

MOVE X'0689' TO IN-BUFFER-SIZE-X

Just to make sure we understand each other, if you got a hex dump of your pgm
after the move, IN-BUFFER-SIZE would contain '0689'. The decimal equivilant
would be '1673'.   







Jim Reinhart <jreinhart1@cox.net> wrote in message news:<B8DFF611.5816%jreinhart1@cox.net>...
> in article a9adte$r9v$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net, William M. Klein at
> wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com wrote on 4/13/02 4:10 PM:
…[39 more quoted lines elided]…
> COMP is COMP is COMP.  It is all binary 2 bytes, 4 bytes or 8 bytes.
```

<a id="t-b6696187a3"></a>
## Thread: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"? — 5 this month / 7 total

_Topics: [`Off-topic and spam`](topics.md#spam)_

_Thread started [2002-03](2002-03.md#t-b6696187a3)._

#### ↳ Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

- **From:** j6vflbl6vy6g8o001@sneakemail.com (Kevin Arthur)
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T14:17:41-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<e87297f1.0204121317.318d8989@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<a7o5d1$4jga$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <3C9F9F26.5A0A6B16@shaw.ca>`

```
> bgwillia@vcu.edu wrote (in an original post which doesn't seem to be there anymore, at least on google)...
> 
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> > Boyce Williams

Okay:

- In the first place, a resume is not the place to explain hows and
wheres of why you changed employment; it's simply a list of
experience.

- On your resume, simply list the beginning and ending dates
(month/year all that's necessary), the position, and the employer, and
of course your accomplishments/responsibilities in that position, etc.
 Usual stuff.  DO NOT attempt to explain WHY you left one employer for
another.  Not relevant here.

- Remember: All a resume is is a tool to get you the INTERVIEW! 
Nothing more.

- Note that a potential NEW employer is NOT going to see your
application forms or personnel file from the previous.  So they will
not see that phrase there.

- If you get the interview, the first thing that's going to stand out,
and the most relevant issue is that you were with your former employer
for 13 YEARS!  That's pretty darn good.

- Question: Did you survive numerous downsizings and cutbacks?  Or is
this the first time the company has faced an adverse business climate?
 If the former, that's a good sign - the company kept you over the
other folks it deemed to let go on prior occasions.  That meant you
were considered more valuable.  (Usually, but my no means always, of
course).  If this is the first real cutback they've done, and you're
one of the first to go, that's the time to ask yourself (and your
former supervisor if you can) what you could have done better so
they'd have wanted to keep you over the others.

- As far as your question about the employer prior to that, if it were
to come up, you could HONESTLY say something like:

"Well, the company was going in a direction I was concerned about.  I
expressed my reservations about what I saw happening around me to my
supervisor, who - much to my surprise - took great exception to my
being so frank with him.  I was considering leaving anyway, so we
mutually agreed that I probably should do so."

This is true, right?  If you were that unhappy with your boss at the
time, I'm sure you WERE considering leaving.  I'm sure you mutually
agreed that that was a good thing.  If pressed on the issue of
resignation vs. firing, you can honestly say:

"Well, after the above discussion with my supervisor, I planned on
submitting my resignation, but wasn't given the chance.  They just
asked me to leave."

(i.e. "fired you" without actually saying those words.)  Question is:
Is this true?  If you had the kind of conversation with your boss that
I imagine you did, you probably WERE planning on leaving.

Anyway, that was 13 years ago.  Your ex-supervisor probably isn't
there any more himself, and your personnel file has probably long
since been archived in some small, dark, dusty room.  Doubtful that
due dilligence will extend back that far.

- Now, as one who has dealt with troublesome subordinates myself,
since I'm helping you get your next job, I would like to clarify:

No boss should be so insecure as to fire you for disagreeing with him.
 Depending on how you do it, of course.  Expressing reservations or
concerns is one thing.  Arguing at length, or in front of others is
another.  And yet another thing is because you disagree, to refuse to
do what you're instructed to do at all.

The first is good: Varied viewpoints are always welcome.  No one
should be surrounded by "yes men".
The second isn't good.  I'll put up with an argument a few times. 
Then it gets old.
The latter I won't put up with at all.  If this is what you did, I'd
have fired you too.

Anyway, good luck with your search.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

> Depending on how you do it, of course.  Expressing reservations or
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
>should be surrounded by "yes men".

Mr Arthur, with all due respect... if this were the case then there might 
not be nearly as much humor found in the quotation attributed to Samuel 
Goldwyn of -

'I don't want yes-men around me. I want everyone to tell the truth, even 
if it costs them their jobs.'

... or, as my Sainted Mother - may she sleep with the angels - told me 
just before I started working at my first Real Job - 

'When it comes to work just remember two things: you can be wrong about 
something... and be fired for it, you can be right about something... and 
be fired for it.'

DD

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-04-13T10:29:15-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a99fbr$pm1$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<a7o5d1$4jga$1@mercury.vcu.edu> <e87297f1.0204121317.318d8989@posting.google.com> <a981b2$rm1$1@panix1.panix.com> <3CB7C824.A5E8BEFD@dontspam.optonline.net>`

```
In article <3CB7C824.A5E8BEFD@dontspam.optonline.net>,
Liam Devlin  <LiamD@nospam.optonline.net> wrote:
>docdwarf@panix.com wrote:

[snippage]

>> ... or, as my Sainted Mother - may she sleep with the angels - told me
>> just before I started working at my first Real Job -
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>heart to see that corporate America is maintaining our proud traditions.

Hey, I have nothing against 'employment at will' as long as it works both 
ways.  I've received contracts which contained the 'standard' clause along 
the lines of 'We can fire you in an instant but if you terminate without 
our permission you have to pay us for the remaining term of the contract'; 
I've notified folks that the clause is unacceptable and that if they want 
me they need to draw up a new contract with the clause equalised... either 
I get the same no-notice privelege or they have to pay me on early 
termination.  (In the World of Theatre this is known as a 'pay or play 
contract')

They always just strike out the clause - one *cannot* give Equal Status to 
a mere consultant! - and there have been times when I've had the need to 
dump someone while there was still work on the table but after all efforts 
to resolve a difficulty/dispute had failed.  Someone *always* says 'You 
can't do that, there's a clause in your contract!'... and when it gets 
pointed out that this clause isn't there someone murfles and blurfles 
about 'corporate loyalty' or 'professional behavior'.

Works both ways.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Off-topic: Rephrasing "employment terminated"?

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** Liam Devlin <LiamD@dontspam.optonline.net>

- **Message-ID:** `<3CB925BB.ACAE6F@dontspam.optonline.net>`

```
docdwarf@panix.com wrote:
> 
> In article <3CB7C824.A5E8BEFD@dontspam.optonline.net>,
…[23 more quoted lines elided]…
> contract')

As a contractor you have that option, employees do not have contracts.
 
> They always just strike out the clause - one *cannot* give Equal Status to
> a mere consultant! - and there have been times when I've had the need to
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> about 'corporate loyalty' or 'professional behavior'.

I hope you laughed long & loud when this was trotted out. The way
corporations have behaved the past 20-25 years, they're entitled to as
much "loyalty" as they can afford. As soon as they decide they're better
off without you, for any reason or no reason at all, you're toast,
regardless of how long or hard you've worked for them or what you've
accomplished while in their employ.
```

<a id="t-6817911ecd"></a>
## Thread: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file — 32 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** bardsfamily@yahoo.com (Alexa)
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T15:38:00-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com>`

```
This is an old-time coding question.  I am looking for a better way to
add a new inquiry screen.

We have a Vsam file where the records are in the following sequence

Company1 record
company1  division1  record
company1  division1  store1 record
company1  division1  store2 record
company1  division1  store3 record
company1  division2  store2 record
company1  division2  store4 record  
company2 record
company2  division1  record
company2  division1  store1  record
company2  division1  store3  record
company2  division2  record
company2  division2  store1 record


The customers want to be able to see in a single screen all the
divisions with which a given store is associated within a given
company.

So they would see
company1   Store1  division1
                   division2

I cannot figure out an efficient way to code this.  I can only see 2
ways to code this.
1) Read the entire file (which is thousands of records) everytime they
want to access the date.  This is not viable since this is online.
2) On a daily basis, create a new vsam file that is in company, store,
division order and read this instead.  The only real issue here is
that they can add stores and divisions realtime as well as batch so I
will need to update this file realtime as well which means changing
the other production programs which they really do not want me to do.

Does anyone see any other way to accomplish this?  Any suggestions
would be appreciated.

Thanks,

Alex


compan
```

#### ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald_tees@sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T18:44:45-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<OpJt8.18453$qX3.2106163@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com>`

```
Why not just code in a secondary key?  Then when the file is updated, both
key structures are updated.

Donald

"Alexa" <bardsfamily@yahoo.com> wrote in message
news:80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com...
> This is an old-time coding question.  I am looking for a better way to
> add a new inquiry screen.
…[44 more quoted lines elided]…
> compan
```

#### ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-04-12T21:53:19-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9832f$1ap$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com>,
Alexa <bardsfamily@yahoo.com> wrote:
>This is an old-time coding question.  I am looking for a better way to
>add a new inquiry screen.

Oh boy... old-time coding!  Let's see...

>
>We have a Vsam file where the records are in the following sequence
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
>I cannot figure out an efficient way to code this.

That's because... there *isn't* an 'efficient way to code this'; the 
customers are asking for something the system was not designed to do.  It 
seems that this file was designed for a particular batch report and now 
just sort of gets dragged into all manner of inappropriate situations 
because... well, because it is there and doing things right would take Too 
Much Time and Money.

>I can only see 2
>ways to code this.
>1) Read the entire file (which is thousands of records) everytime they
>want to access the date.  This is not viable since this is online.

Excellent analysis.

>2) On a daily basis, create a new vsam file that is in company, store,
>division order and read this instead.  The only real issue here is
>that they can add stores and divisions realtime as well as batch so I
>will need to update this file realtime as well which means changing
>the other production programs which they really do not want me to do.

Also... creating a single file for a single application is a practise to 
be avoided.

>
>Does anyone see any other way to accomplish this?  Any suggestions
>would be appreciated.

All righty... actually you don't need *all* the division-level entries, 
just the 18 or so that would fill the detail-line area of your screen (24 
lines per screen minus three lines for header, two lines for footer, one 
line for error-msgs and maybe a couple of blank lines 
here-and-there for 'eye-ease').  Assuming a full 18 lines, though, this 
can be accomplished in a maximum of 54 I-O operations.

Your program is passed *something*, I hope... a company, company and store 
or company, store and division.  Whatever you get you move to your RIDFLD 
and do a START NOT < and a READ NEXT; the least you will get will be a 
company record.  If you get a company record (blank store and division) 
you do another READ NEXT and you have a division record.  These are the 
three I-Os you'll have to do to get each first division record for a given 
store.

Anyhow, after you have the first one how do you get to the next?  
Ahhhhh... take the last character of the division field that your read 
returned (reference-modification makes this a trivial task) and MOVE it to 
the second position of a two-character alpha field which you have 
REDEFINED as a binary field... my memory, admittedly porous, has it as 
something like

01  WS-FLDS.
    05  WS-CHARFLD.
        10  WS-CHAR1 PIC X.
        10  WS-CHAR2 PIC X.
    05  BINFLD REDEFINES WS-CHARFLD PIC 9(4) COMP.

... or something like that, so your code becomes

MOVE DIVISIONFLD(LENGTH OF DIVISIONFLD:1) TO WS-CHAR2.
ADD 1 TO BINFLD.
MOVE WS-CHAR-2 TO DIVISIONFLD(LENGTH OF DIVISIONFLD:1).

... and then you do your I-Os again, START NOT <, READ NEXT (and check if 
a new company rec is there, if so another READ NEXT).

(using a binary field to increment an alpha is an Anciente, Dirtye 
Techniquee... and someone is bound to ask where you learned it from 
because *nobody* uses such things any more)

DD
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T18:42:58+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Sg_u8.80484$_U1.4284537904@newssvr30.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <a9832f$1ap$1@panix1.panix.com>`

```

<docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:a9832f$1ap$1@panix1.panix.com...
[snip]
> Anyhow, after you have the first one how do you get to the next?
> Ahhhhh... take the last character of the division field that your read
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> something like
[snip]
> (using a binary field to increment an alpha is an Anciente, Dirtye
> Techniquee... and someone is bound to ask where you learned it from
> because *nobody* uses such things any more)

But Doc, that's because it is not necessary, at least in this case.

Assuming the key looks something like:

05  the-prime-key.
      07  company-id         pic X(10).
      07  division-id          pic X(10).
      07  store-id               pic X(10).

after reading the division record, one simply codes:
   MOVE HIGH-VALUES TO DIVISION-ID, STORE-ID.
   START THE-FILE KEY GREATER THAN the-prime-key...
   READ THE-FILE NEXT...
to read the next company record, bypassing the other division and store
record(s).

Tom Morrison
Liant Software Corporation
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T15:58:39-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9hvpf$2nb$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <a9832f$1ap$1@panix1.panix.com> <Sg_u8.80484$_U1.4284537904@newssvr30.news.prodigy.com>`

```
In article <Sg_u8.80484$_U1.4284537904@newssvr30.news.prodigy.com>,
Tom Morrison <t.morrison@liant.com> wrote:
>
><docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:a9832f$1ap$1@panix1.panix.com...
…[26 more quoted lines elided]…
>record(s).

Hmmmmm... this caused me to review the original posting, which stated:

--begin quoted text:

>Company1 record
>company1  division1  record
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…
>                   division2

--end quoted text

... and now I must admit befuddlement.  In the first it seems that 
store(n) is subordinate to division(n) is subordinate to company(n); the 
'wanna see' scenario appears to have division(m) subordinate to store(m) 
subordinate to company(m).

This is odd.  Consider:

Company1 = Amalgamated Stuff
Company1 Division1 = Amalgamated Stuff Ironworks
Company1 Division1 Store1 = Amalgamated Stuff Ironworks Smithville
Company1 Division1 Store2 = Amalgamated Stuff Ironworks Jonesville
Company1 Division2 = Amalgamated Stuff Bakedgoods
Company1 Division2 Store1 = Amalgamated Stuff Bakedgoods Billytowne
Company1 Division2 Store2 = Amalgamated Stuff Bakedgoods Barbatowne

... which would yield a 'wanna see' of:

Amalgamated Stuff Smithville Ironworks
                             Bakedgoods

... which, upon Great Reflection, makes little sense, unless Smithville is 
selling both wrought-iron and kaiser rolls.

Perhaps the original poster might clarify?

DD
```

Sorry about the delay, but I haven't been able to access the newsgroup

Anyway, to try to explain the issue a different way.


The file keeps track of the amount of goods that are sent to each
store by a particular division.  That is why the key is in company,
division, store order.  Now the users would also like to be able to
view all the divisions that send goods to a particular store.  The new
screen would have the company and the store entered.  They then want
to see all the divisions that send goods to that store, so in effect,
they want to see the data in company, store, division order.  I
thought about setting up an alternate index, but I need to do more
research on the impact.
I'm a little rusty on programming, but due to layoffs I am having to
do more so I am hoping someone will have an idea of a more efficient
way to do this.

Thanks for your help.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-04-22T09:02:47-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <B8DFFC4D.5819%jreinhart1@cox.net> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com>,
Alexa <bardsfamily@yahoo.com> wrote:

[snippette]

>The file keeps track of the amount of goods that are sent to each
>store by a particular division.  That is why the key is in company,
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>way to do this.

So let me see if I have this straight now.  At present you have (assuming 
an identifier of ten alphanumeric characters):

01  FILE-REC.
    05  FILE-KEY01.
        10  COMPANY-ID   PIC X(10).
        10  DIVISION-ID  PIC X(10).
        10  STORE-ID     PIC X(10).

... and what you want is ...

    05  FILE-KEY02.
        10  COMPANY-ID   PIC X(10).
        10  STORE-ID     PIC X(10).
        10  DIVISION-ID  PIC X(10).

... and you want a solution which has no impact on the current file 
structure, the other programs which use the file, is fast enough for 
online queries and can be implemented by a 'rusty' programmer because all 
the coders who know their trade have been fired?

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-22T16:43:34+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3cc431b2_10@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <B8DFFC4D.5819%jreinhart1@cox.net> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com> <aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com>`

```
While I think this is a truly beautiful statement of the situation, Doc, it
doesn't help Alexa who has been tasked with doing it...<G> (After all, it
isn't HER fault that things have come to the pass they have...)

Alexa, the real solution to this is to move the VSAM rubbish onto a
Relational Database which supports Company, Division, and Store Objects.
Your Users can then organise and analyse this information in any of the
possible ways they might want to....and they can call off their own Queries
and Reports.

However, that is not going to be practical in the circumstances you
describe, so your best solution is to define an Alternate Index on the VSAM
KSDS. You do this with IDCAMS. Simply set an AI that has the
Company>Store>Division sequence. If you are unfamiliar with it, seek advice
from someone who knows it, and learn something at the same time...

The impacts are the setting up of the new index, the loading of it, and the
amendment of JCL for existing jobs that reference the Dataset, where/if
required. There is a performance hit, as the new index must be updated
whenever the dataset is updated. If updating occurs on-line this hit will
not be noticeable; if it occurs in Batch, the overnight run may be
prolonged.

If I were you, I'd look for another job <G>. The people who are currently
employing you are short-sighted to the point of incompetence and it is only
a matter of time before things collapse, the system is moved to SAP (or
similar) and you are unemployed...

Pete.

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com> <aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com> <3cc431b2_10@Usenet.com> <aa1met$s1a$1@panix1.panix.com>`

<docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:aa1met$s1a$1@panix1.panix.com...
>
> >your best solution is to define an Alternate Index on the VSAM
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>
It is now around 15 years since I last saw an IDCAMS manual and I don't
currently have acccess to one. However, I seem to recall that split keys
were allowed with VSAM KSDS for both primary and secondary indexes. Or maybe
that was SORT...<G>? If the secondary index must be comprised of a single
contiguous key as you describe, then the file will have to change radically.
Kind of defeats the whole idea of alternate indexes, doesn't it?

My Fujitsu COBOL Manual says the following:
(Where "data-name-1" is the name of an Alternate Index field...)

"6. When two or more data-name-1 descriptions are specified, they
are linked in the sequence in which they were written and treated
as a single alternate record key."

I believe this is a Fujitsu extension to the COBOL standard, so it is
probably true that while Fujitsu supports multiple split Alternate keys and
treats them as one key, standard (mainframe (?)) COBOL probably doesn't.

Thanks for pointing out this flaw in my solution...<G>

Pete.
