###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-04-23T12:11:02-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aa412m$ek1$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <3cc431b2_10@Usenet.com> <aa1met$s1a$1@panix1.panix.com> <3cc57797_1@Usenet.com>`

```
In article <3cc57797_1@Usenet.com>,
Peter E. C. Dashwood <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz> wrote:
>
><docdwarf@panix.com> wrote in message news:aa1met$s1a$1@panix1.panix.com...
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
>currently have acccess to one.

<http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com:80/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/DGT1I211/CCONTENTS>

You do now! Waw haw haw haw!

>However, I seem to recall that split keys
>were allowed with VSAM KSDS for both primary and secondary indexes. Or maybe
>that was SORT...<G>? If the secondary index must be comprised of a single
>contiguous key as you describe, then the file will have to change radically.

Not necessarily 'radically'.  My guess is that there isn't enough FILLER 
at the end of a record to allow for the fields needed so the LRECL would 
have to be increased.  Once that's done then a FileAid step (or 
SYNCSORT/DFSORT - hi, Mr Jaeger! - with INFIL/OUTFIL manipulations) would 
need to be built into the nightly REORG job; the new step would populate 
the new portion of the record with the desired fields from the present 
record... and then the AIX built on this new field.

>Kind of defeats the whole idea of alternate indexes, doesn't it? 

Not at all.  PRIMKEY of CUSTNO, ALTKEY of LASTNAM+FIRSTNAM+MI (all 
contiguous fields) is an Anciente and Honrablee Construct.

But... back to split AIXs on a VSAM... according to:

<http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com:80/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/DGT1I211/13.1.2?SHELF=&DT=20001016161533>

--begin quoted text:

KEYS(length offset| 64 0) 
describes the alternate-key field in the base cluster's data record. 

The key field of an alternate index is called an alternate key. The data 
record's alternate key can overlap or be contained entirely within another 
(alternate or prime) key field. 

The length plus offset cannot be greater than the length of the base 
cluster's data record. 

When the base cluster's data record spans control intervals, the record's 
alternate-key field is within the record's first segment (that is, in the 
first control interval). 

length offset 
gives the length of the alternate key (between 1 and 255), in bytes, and 
its displacement from the beginning of the base cluster's data record, in 
bytes. 

--end quoted text

... which does *not* say a key cannot contain noncontiguous fields... but 
to describe something as 'length, offset' does not, in my experience, 
allow for anything *but* contiguous data.

[Fujitsu snippidu]

>Thanks for pointing out this flaw in my solution...<G>

Well, when the theory and the facts do not coincide... change the facts!

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

_(thread depth: 8)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-23T13:07:07-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aa4821$in8$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com> <aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com> <3cc431b2_10@Usenet.com> <aa1met$s1a$1@panix1.panix.com> <3cc57797_1@Usenet.com> <3CC59E53.D7C41402@shaw.ca>`

```
It *is* included in the draft Standard - and will be "official" come
December 2002 (probably). See the

    ALTERNATE RECORD KEY ...record-key-name-1 SOURCE IS { data-name-2 } ...

syntax.  (Naturally <G> it doesn't match either the Micro Focus or Fujitsu
syntax - but the functionality is there)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

_(thread depth: 8)_

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-25T10:59:28+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3cc884d0_6@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com> <aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com> <3cc431b2_10@Usenet.com> <aa1met$s1a$1@panix1.panix.com> <3cc57797_1@Usenet.com> <CRDx8.43763$Rw2.3406273@bgtnsc05-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```
No Warren, you're not dreaming.

The problem is that, while you can "assemble" the key in working-storage,
unless the SAME key is held on the FILE you will not be able to access it...

The file would therefore have to be modified to accommodate the new
alternate key. And then the new key field would have to be loaded...

Pete.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-22T22:29:36+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<k90x8.13228$t65.1072@nwrddc02.gnilink.net>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <B8DFFC4D.5819%jreinhart1@cox.net> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com> <aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com> <80ae94d9.0204221211.53547d8e@posting.google.com>`

```
I missed the volume (number of records), but if it aint too big....

01  FILLER
   05 BEEN-HERE-SW    PIC X(01) VALUE 'N'.
      88 BEEN-HERE  VALUE IS 'Y'.

PROCEDURE DIVISIION...

 IF NOT BEEN_HERE
    SET BEEN-HERE TO TRUE
    PERFORM CREATE-COMPANY-STORE-DIVISION-KEY
END-IF

CREATE-COMPANY-STORE-DIVISION-KEY.
  Read the input file sequentially, RELEASE the records to a SORT, and
RETURN them into a table thus:
 01 FILLER
    05  COMPANY-DIVISION-STORE-KEY OCCUR  "Lots" INDEXED BY CDS-IDX
        10 KEY
            15 COMPANY  PIC X()
            15 STORE       PIC X...
         10 DIVISION   PIC X()  OCCURS Number-of-divisions TIMES
            15 Division-Fact  PICTURE whatever

If you can hold this table in memory, you can do a SEARCH ALL given Company
and Store when you need to lookup info that way.

You can play around with this depending on any limitations on company,
division or store number (i.e., ranges in which they occur).  I left this
table sorted so you might SEARCH ALL if you are able to limit, say, "store
number" to a discrete set of values. I have to believe ten (10) SEARCH ALL
(binary) would be faster than 10 SEARCH  (linear).

What this does is lets you read the whole file sequentially ONCE at the
beginning of the program run and do all the rest of the lookups in memory.
The first user might find it slow, but once the table is loaded should be
OK.

Hell, it's a thought...

MCM
```

>From: docdwarf@panix.com

>Michael Mattias <michael.mattias@gte.net> wrote:

>>> >  Read the input file sequentially, RELEASE the records to a SORT, and

>>> I believe, Mr Mattias, that this is for an online application.

Well we have one monstrosity on-line that does a sort in linkage.  I guess it's
a bubble sort (never done such thing myself so I'm not going to swear to it). 
All kinds of pointers and indexes and 'stuff'.  

Users ask 'can you make this one change in....' and we go screaming from the
building.  Our boss rounds us up, locks us in a room and we draw straws, short
straw 'wins'?  After making the change and getting the user's blessing, putting
into production and having it crash, yanking it out and 'fixing' it, the victim
is allowed a nervous and mental day - sometimes.
```

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aa3bsd$fij$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com> <aa11ln$ep8$1@panix1.panix.com> <80ae94d9.0204221211.53547d8e@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <80ae94d9.0204221211.53547d8e@posting.google.com>,
Alexa <bardsfamily@yahoo.com> wrote:
>Not fire!  Laid off! :-)

Well... that makes *all* the difference, doesn't it?

>The few that hadn't already moved on to
>coding in PC languages anyway. :-(

If one has the right tools then COBOL is a PC language.

>
>I can do the code changes as they are not that difficult. I was just
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>see if I am missing something obvious.

So far it seems that I, for one, missed something obvious in your 
statement of the problem... but by all means, sound away.  The only 
suggestion thus far has been for an AIX but I am still uncertain how that 
can be done without the altkey's data already being contiguous.

>If I have to modify several
>programs, and/or create a new file, then I will do so.  I thought I
>would see if someone had a slicker way to do it since I have been
>impressed by most people's knowledge on this board and their
>willingness to help.

'Willing' help is one thing... 'helpful' help another.  I've just spent 
several months on my own job looking to develop a method to balance an 
accounting/billing system that's quite the Black Box... stuff goes in, 
stuff goes out and that's about all anyone knows.

The two most 'helpful' suggestions - ones which cause me weeks of work to 
show how *wrong* they were - went something like:

'Hey, think of it like a cash-register.  Take yesterday's close-out total 
of unbilled charges, subtract all of today's unbilled charges, add all of 
today's unbilled charges and you'll get today's close-out unbilled 
balance.'

(note - I'se jes' a COBOL-codin' fool, that is why it took me weeks to 
demonstrate the essential flaw in this)

'Hey... you don't need to take a half-hour to balance across the DB2 
tables; all ya gotta do is make a new file to hold yesterday's balance, 
get today's transactions, apply them to that balance and you'll have 
today's balance.'

(this time being a COBOL-codin' fool allowed me to see the flaw in this 
nigh immediately... but there have been months of fighting the 'Well, I 
don't see why you just don't institute that running-balance file that was 
mentioned...')

>
>If anyone has a suggestion, then I would like to hear it. If no one
>has a suggestion, then I am just going to presume that I am  not as
>"rusty" as I thought and continue on! Might get a swelled head this
>way!

Hey, it is the season to get some new hats, anyways.

DD

>
>
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
>> DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Trouble accessing records on VSAM file

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-22T17:44:24+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CC44B7F.B63514F5@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<80ae94d9.0204121437.5971b70f@posting.google.com> <B8DFFC4D.5819%jreinhart1@cox.net> <80ae94d9.0204220341.32a92114@posting.google.com>`

```


Alexa wrote:

> Anyway, to try to explain the issue a different way.
>
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Others have suggested ways you might tackle this, but it's largely a guessing game, because
although you've indicated what's wanted., you haven't shown us your current record layout.

You didn't indicate in your messages - is this information taken from a 'Summary File', or as you
indicated 'thousands' of records, are you accessing the query directly from a Transaction File,
itemized for each delivery/stock transfer ?

Is this *unit* inventory control or just a *dollar amount* query. If it IS inventory, whichever
solution you come up with, don't be surprised if they subsequently tell you they  want some
viewing by Product/SKU numbers or UPC  - so bear this in mind with whatever solution you come up
with.

Something else which might throw a monkey wrench - assume for discussion you are in the
supermarket business. Individual 'merchandisers', will want to know about specific products, and
that throws in the necessity of Product Groups, e.g., dairy products, (further broken down into
Sub-Product groups, say, butter, margarine, milk, cream, yogurts etc.), frozen foods, fresh
veggies, dry goods (cleaning materials, stationery, magazines etc.).

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

---

## Thread: MAINFRAME/ DB2/ DBA/ CHI — 14 msgs

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe) · [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### MAINFRAME/ DB2/ DBA/ CHI

- **From:** "Tom Gugger" <omnimed@norden1.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-13T03:00:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a98706$1og$0@192.153.35.30>`

```


Subject: DB2/  DBA/  CHICAGO
Date: Saturday, April 13, 2002 10:45 PM

                                                             OMNI  GROUP
                                                           tgugger@msn.com
                                                              419-893-6334

    OMNI GROUP is currently searching for a DB2 DBA.  This person will have
10 years
of Data Processing Experience. Technology experience MUST encompass COBOL,
DB2
for OS/390, DB2 for MVS/ESA, SQL and modeling products.
    This position is located in Chicago, one must be available for a July 1,
or sooner start.
The salary is 55K and is for one year, with an option for two additional
years. No living
expenses are furnished. Any travel associated with the actual job of course
will be reimbursed.
    If Interested--Email(tgugger@msn.com) or call 419-893-6334.


Thanks,

Tom Gugger
```

#### ↳ Re: MAINFRAME/ DB2/ DBA/ CHI

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-04-13T10:22:45-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a99evl$p6v$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<a98706$1og$0@192.153.35.30>`

```

[posted and emailed]

In article <a98706$1og$0@192.153.35.30>,
Tom Gugger <omnimed@norden1.com> wrote:

All right... let me see if I can show what the bones reveal.

>
>
>Subject: DB2/  DBA/  CHICAGO
>Date: Saturday, April 13, 2002 10:45 PM

[snip]

>    OMNI GROUP is currently searching for a DB2 DBA.  This person will have
>10 years
>of Data Processing Experience. Technology experience MUST encompass COBOL,
>DB2
>for OS/390, DB2 for MVS/ESA, SQL and modeling products.

DB2, DBA, COBOL.  Note the use of 'SQL'; this is, to my unpractised, 
similar to a stinkwad pimp who says, in doubt, 'I see your resume shows 20 
years experience with VSAM files but I don't see any mention of 
IDCAMS...'

(for non-IBM mainframe folks: this is the equivalent of saying 'I see your 
resume shows 20 years experience driving a Volkswagen Beetle but I don't 
see any mention of being able to handle a stickshift...')

>    This position is located in Chicago, one must be available for a July 1,
>or sooner start.

So... advertising 13 Apr for 1 Jul start.  Again, in my experience, this 
sort of lead-time is... rare; my experience is that clients sit with their 
thumbs in their... noses and then scream at a pimp 'We need someone to 
correct all our errors of the past six months and we need him now Now 
NOW!'.

>The salary is 55K and is for one year, with an option for two additional
>years.

US$55K... and these seem to be contract limitations; perm slots rarely 
mention 'two additional years'.

Based on the standard 40hr/wk 50wk/yr model this works out to US$27.50/hr, 
give or take a hair.

>No living
>expenses are furnished. Any travel associated with the actual job of course
>will be reimbursed.

So, to recapitulate the analysis... 10 year DB2 DBA contract in a major 
market, long lead-time on the start and a rate of US$27.50/hr.

My conclusion?  Someone is looking to import labor but has to show a 
'good-faith effort' in finding it locally; they put out an ad like this 
knowing full well that they would be lucky to find a 10 year DB2 DBA at 
*double* the offered rate to cover themselves in case of investigation.

DD
```

-Paul

Wow... I posted and emailed my analysis of this and I got an emailed 
reply... since I responded to the emailed reply I will follow up here, 
courting accusations of Bad Taste, by posting my reply to Mr Gugger's 
email to me.

NOTE - I have written things in this which might be seen as being 
less-than-courteous to incest survivors, alcoholics, the products of 
inbreeding and those who have intimate preferences other than mine.  These 
things might seem, when take out of context, insulting... when read in the 
present context, however, they may well be seen as appropriate restraint.

--begin quoted text

From docdwarf@panix.com Sat Apr 13 19:38:16 2002
Date: Sat, 13 Apr 2002 19:37:57 -0400 (EDT)
From: docdwarf@panix.com
To: Tom Gugger <TGugger@msn.com>

> (You can not teach an OLD dog new tricks) I have NEVER
> in 15yrs placed an H1.

How lovely for you.

> (I am a VN Vet)

The Army needed folks to clean latrines, sure... oh, I'm sorry, I
descended to the kind of cheap, mindless, blathering insult with which you
began this email.

> In the the few hours this position has been posted, I
> have received several qualified resumes from US Citizens. One is local.

I see... and you take pity on these 'set-in-their way' mainframers?  How
very generous of you to deal with such folks.

> Neither
> has worked for four months or longer.

Someone sure learned A Job in the Army... clean latrines *and* deal with
the trash!

>One would "just like to have some stability to finish off my career"

Maybe he'll find it when he sobers up... or are you looking for a
drinking-buddy?

>I expect five or more qualified resumes in the next several days. To most
>of these people this position represents a 55k raise.

Someone has to inhabit the low end of the salary-curve... nice to see
you've found someone there to join you, as well!

>Possibly you and your family were of the type that food stamps and
>welfare is a more lucrative appeal if you do not get just what you want.

No more possible than your parents being blood-relatives... no, wait,
given the quality of your response... as you were, soldier.

>Fortunately there are some of us left, who look at a situation and make
>the most of it.

And if this situation is, for you and your clients, 'the most of it'
then... bona fortuna!

>(I hope you do not have children--or if you do, let"s
>hope your divorced)

My progeny are beyond the capabilities of your imagining... but then
again, most imagining of progeny are done by heterosexuals, no?

DD

DB2 DBA, COBOL and systems analysis?  First of all, DB2 DBAs create, set a
ton of access options and create and optimize the database BUT don't write
code or analyze the business.  COBOL programmer analysts understand how to
use DB2 and analyze the business but don't function as a DBA.  Modeling
should be done by a systems or business analyst but they don't code and the
definitely don't optimize the database.  I am getting sick of people that
don't know the difference.

```
hi,
I have a question again about screens in miscosoft cobol 4.5.

How can I change the color of some tekst on the screen by clicking an arrow
button?
I'd like to make some kind of a select box to choose between three options.
Actually, I'm obliged to do that :(
It's thus not a field tekst that should change color, just some tekst that
appears on the screen, from which I know the rows it's in, and the items are
all 15 characters of width.

thanks in advance,

  tomba
```

#### ↳ Re: screen color

- **From:** "Donald Tees" <donald_tees@sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-13T12:33:49-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<%3Zt8.22099$AX.4320847@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<3cb85429$0$8140$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
MS Cobol 4.5 can not change the colour of the screen using the standard
Cobol.  You can only change colours at the source code level.

Donald

#### ↳ Re: screen color

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T08:59:19+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB936D7.A39D3171@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3cb85429$0$8140$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
Tomba wrote:

> I have a question again about screens in miscosoft cobol 4.5.
> 
> How can I change the color of some tekst on the screen by clicking an arrow
> button?

You can change the display colour using CALL x"A7" see Operating Guide
page D-13.

I use this for all colouration in MF/MS as I allow the users to set
their colour codes for each system/company/terminal combination which I
store away and reload when they log into my systems.  I then just need
to perform routines such as Screen-HL (highlight) or Screen-BG (normal
background text) and it retrieves and sets the colour combination that
the user has chosen.  

In your case you would need to detect the arrow keys in CRT Status field
and then set the colour for each selection and redisplay them.
        
        ...
        CRT STATUS IS CRT-Status
        ...
        01   Selection-Colours.
             03  Select-Attr         PIC X OCCURS 3.
        ...
        MOVE ALL Colour-Normal   TO Selection-Colours
        MOVE 1                   TO Selection-Index
        MOVE Colour-Highlight    TO Selection-Attr(1)
        MOVE ZERO                TO Selection-Made
        MOVE 0416                TO Selection-Cusor
        PERFORM
            UNTIL Selection-Made > ZERO

            CALL x"A7" USING Set-Colour Select-Attr(1)
            DISPLAY Select-Text-1       AT 0416
            CALL x"A7" USING Set-Colour Select-Attr(2)
            DISPLAY Select-Text-2       AT 0431
            CALL x"A7" USING Set-Colour Select-Attr(3)
            DISPLAY Select-Text-3       AT 0449

            ACCEPT Selection-Field      AT Selection-Cursor
            IF ( Key-Stroke = RIGHT-ARROW 
             AND Selection-Index < 3 )
                MOVE Colour-Normal      TO
Selection-Attr(Selection-Index)
                ADD 1                   TO Selection-Index
                MOVE Colour-Highlight   TO
Selection-Attr(Selection-Index)
                ADD 15                  TO Selection-Cursor
            ELSE
            IF ( Key-Stroke = LEFT-ARROW 
             AND Selection-Index > 1 )
                MOVE Colour-Normal      TO
Selection-Attr(Selection-Index)
                SUBTRACT 1            FROM Selection-Index
                MOVE Colour-Highlight   TO
Selection-Attr(Selection-Index)
                SUBTRACT 15           FROM Selection-Cursor
            ELSE
            IF ( Key-Stroke = ENTER-KEY )
                MOVE Selection-index    TO Selection-Made
            END-IF
            END-IF
            END-IF
        END-PERFORM
                
Of course you would want to made this somewhat more generalised.
```

…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> > thank you

Upper Iowa University (http://uiu.edu) offers online courses in
OO-COBOL (MIS210) and Windows API COBOL. UIU's online Bachelor of
Science and Master of Business Administration degrees are designed to
give you the flexibility you need to balance classwork with career
work.
  
The COBOL courses (the NetExpress University edition) are for new
programmers as well as for "legacy" COBOL programmers who want to
learn object oriented COBOL and Windows API programming in COBOL (3
college credit hours each).  The classes are conducted asynchronously
via the Internet so that they are open to anyone at anywhere in the
world.

<a id="t-7b5dfa7a42"></a>
## Thread: Attempting to read variable length records as raw data — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Attempting to read variable length records as raw data

- **From:** broom@voicenet.com (broom)
- **Date:** 2002-04-13T11:33:03-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<c948eb61.0204131033.17dcbe06@posting.google.com>`

```
I've got 3490 EBCDIC tapes that were produced on an IBM S/390 
box.

The language that created them is COBOL, and the layout shows 
multiple "occurs" fields at the end of the record.

I'm told that these are variable length records, and if the 
tailend occurs fields are not used, then they won't be stored 
on tape.

Looking at the tape shows be the 1st record starts with
3E 26 00 00 02 DF 00 00 and then the actual data (I think),
and then all other records have a 4 byte prefix which seems
to vary.

02 DF 00 00 seems common, as it 97 60 00 00 and others.
The record size is LRECL (max, I guess) of 7261, but it
should be a minimum of 277 (which is the end of the last
non-occurs field).

Doing a "big-endian" unsigned 16 bit dump of the 02 DF
gives me 735, which is 40 bytes into the next record so
that can't be right.

Is there any reference that can show me how to dump this
data?

Thanks.

Please email, as well as post, and I will summarize.

Thanks.

Barry
```

#### ↳ Re: Attempting to read variable length records as raw data

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-13T18:21:44-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9aeif$83m$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<c948eb61.0204131033.17dcbe06@posting.google.com>`

```
It would help us if you could show us the JCL that created the tape file.
For the moment, I will assume that it is a VB (Variable blocked) format
file.  If so, there is a "block descriptor word" at the beginning of each
block and then a record descriptor word at the beginning of each record.
(This would explain the 1st record's prefix being "different" from later
ones - at least until you reach the next block).

Can you tell us what operating system and "tools" you are using to "dump"
this file?  If you have COBOL on the "receiving system" - the chances are
that this would be your easiest method of reading the file.

I think that before anyone can tell you exactly how to "interpret" your
file, we will need to know more about how it was created and where/how you
are trying to "dump" it.
```

#### ↳ Re: Attempting to read variable length records as raw data

- **From:** Liam Devlin <LiamD@dontspam.optonline.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T07:13:32+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB92C0F.F3D8B48B@dontspam.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<c948eb61.0204131033.17dcbe06@posting.google.com>`

```
broom wrote:
> 
> I've got 3490 EBCDIC tapes that were produced on an IBM S/390
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
> that can't be right.

The language isn't the determining factor on IBM mf's, it's a function
of the access method. This looks like straightforward VB QSAM. The
length of records is stored by QSAM as LLbb, i.e., a 2-byte binary
length followed by the "bb" field, typically binary zeroes, called a
Record Descriptor Word (RDW). The "LL" includes the length of the RDW. 

A block of VB data looks like:

RDW = block length, so this block is 15,910 bytes long (max LRECL is
probably something like 16,000 or 16,384. 
The first logical record begins at the 5th byte and is 735 bytes long.
Its RDW is located at start of block +4 bytes. 

The 2nd logical record is located at the beginning of the block+4+735,
and so on. This logical record contains 731 bytes of data.

(offsets are relative to one)

HTH
```

#### ↳ Re: Attempting to read variable length records as raw data

- **From:** Liam Devlin <LiamD@dontspam.optonline.net>
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T07:13:45+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CB92C1C.83DC06C0@dontspam.optonline.net>`
- **References:** `<c948eb61.0204131033.17dcbe06@posting.google.com>`

```
broom wrote:
> 
> I've got 3490 EBCDIC tapes that were produced on an IBM S/390
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
> that can't be right.

The language isn't the determining factor on IBM mf's, it's a function
of the access method. This looks like straightforward VB QSAM. The
length of records is stored by QSAM as LLbb, i.e., a 2-byte binary
length followed by the "bb" field, typically binary zeroes, called a
Record Descriptor Word (RDW). The "LL" includes the length of the RDW. 

A block of VB data looks like:

RDW = block length, so this block is 15,910 bytes long (max LRECL is
probably something like 16,000 or 16,384. 
The first logical record begins at the 5th byte and is 735 bytes long.
Its RDW is located at start of block +4 bytes. 

The 2nd logical record is located at the beginning of the block+4+735,
and so on. This logical record contains 731 bytes of data.

(offsets are relative to one)

HTH
```

#### ↳ Re: Attempting to read variable length records as raw data

- **From:** jnjsle@aol.com (Jnjsle)
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T04:37:19+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020415003719.11106.00003782@mb-ca.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<c948eb61.0204131033.17dcbe06@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi Barry,

You don't mention what environment you plan to use this data in or what you
intend to do to it. So I'll assume you don't have mainframe or COBOL resources
at your disposal. 

If you have the capabilty of reading the data  a block at a time, you can use
the info Liam provided to loop thru the block and extract the individual
records. 

The remaining problem you face is the tables at the end of the recs (the
multiple occurs). If you provide a copy of the record layout, that may provide
some clues.

Regards, Jack.
```

Hi Ron,

I commiserate. These "Twilight Zone" problems can dive you up a wall. 
Sometimes the best thing to do is start over from square 1. Throw everything
else away (OK save a copy 1st); sometimes you look right past the error no
matter how many times you look at it. Doing it over may break the loop.

Maybe just code the part of the pgm with the problem and concentrate on that.
The problem may even disappear. You never know, the error may not be where you
think it is.

Sorry we couldn't help and good luck.

Jack

## Thread: Write variable length records.... — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Write variable length records....

- **From:** mikey.unsafe@gmx.net (Mikey)
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T11:41:38-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<26282372.0204141041.70dfc8b6@posting.google.com>`

```
Hey

I have a problem...I use the following structure:

       01 B-Rec.

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                                *
      * Length FixPart      : 34 B                                     *
      *                                                                *
      * Lenght VariablePart :+80 B x Num. of Drivers / Truck           *
      *                                                                *
      *                      +20 B x Num. of ServicePeriod/Driver/Truck*
      *                                                                *
      ******************************************************************
      * FixPart    :                                       fix    34 B *
          05 B-FixedPart.
             15 B-TruckPart       PIC X(32).
      * DriverCounter **************************************************
             15 B-VarDCounter     PIC 9(2).

      * 1. VarPart :                                       max.  800 B *
      *                                                    min.   80 B *

          05 B-VarPart            OCCURS 1 TO 10
                                  DEPENDING ON B-VarFCounter.
             15 B-V-DriverPart    PIC X(78).
             15 B-V-VarDCounter   PIC 9(2).

      * 2. VarPart :                                       max. 2000 B *
      *                                                    min.   20 B *

             15 B-V-ServicePart   OCCURS 1 TO 10
                                  DEPENDING ON TmpDCounter.
                20 B-V-SFrom      PIC 9(10).
                20 B-V-STo        PIC 9(10).

      ******************************************************************


Okay this is 2 level variable Record - I dont know if this is possible
but when I use the B-VarFCounter instead of TmpDCounter the compiler
tells me that I cant use B-VarFCounter as subscript because it is already
in a table...
So if I use TmpDCounter instead of B-VarFCounter and move the value of
TmpDCounter to B-VarFCounter, the last one sometimes has ZERO values.
but it should have the same value as TmpDCounter

Any suggestion how to implement a 2 level variable record ?


thx mike
```

#### ↳ Re: Write variable length records....

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T22:26:26+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CBA0186.E396D5E@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<26282372.0204141041.70dfc8b6@posting.google.com>`

```


Mikey wrote:

> Hey
>
> I have a problem...I use the following structure:

Which compiler are you using  ? The following wouldn't compile for me, (Net
Express)  until I used the ODOSLIDE directive; the compiler would give an
error on the second occurs.

 *>------------------------------------------------------
$set ODOSLIDE

 Program-id. RecVarying.

 Working-Storage Section.

 01 B-VarFCounter              pic 9(03).
 01 TmpDCounter                pic 9(03).

 01 B-Rec.
   05 B-FixedPart.
      15 B-TruckPart       PIC X(32).
      15 B-VarDCounter     PIC 9(2).

   05 B-VarPart            OCCURS 1 TO 10
                           DEPENDING ON B-VarFCounter.
      15 B-V-DriverPart    PIC X(78).
      15 B-V-VarDCounter   PIC 9(2).
      15 B-V-ServicePart   OCCURS 1 TO 10
                           DEPENDING ON TmpDCounter.
         20 B-V-SFrom      PIC 9(10).
         20 B-V-STo        PIC 9(10).

Now before we go further, what's with your counters. As shown in my code the
counters are defined separately as 01 Levels. So what's the purpose of :-

                          15 B-VarDCounter     PIC 9(2).
                          15 B-V-VarDCounter   PIC
9(2).                        ?

Either move values to the 'separate' counters, or if using the 'in-table
counters' move values into them to control the number of current occurs.
Unless you are doing something really tricky, can't see the need for two
pairs of counters.

Now - if your tables were FIXED, both "OCCURS" occurring ten, then you could
move values into the counters to control the active set of occurs, and ensure
you don't do an invalid subscript access e.g. (6, 8) when you have previously
set the occurs to say 3 and 4.

Two the second part of your question. As a general guide it makes sense to
have your counters outside of the table. However, if you were passing the
table to another program with a CALL, then it makes sense to have your
counters INCLUDED in the table - then the receiving program knows what the
counter values are. Alternatively, when CALLING, you could pass both the
external counters and the table (no inclusive counters).

Yours is a fairly simple table, but as an exercise in XP(eXtreme
Programming), when moving values to counters, ENSURE they don't exceed the
maximums you have set - report as an error if this happens.

HTH

Jimmy, Calgary AB




>
>
…[45 more quoted lines elided]…
> thx mike
```

#### ↳ Re: Write variable length records....

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T23:14:15-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9dk8c$6hk$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<26282372.0204141041.70dfc8b6@posting.google.com>`

```
Others have already asked which compiler you are using.  Having an Occurs
Depending ON *nested* within another ODO (Occurs Depending On) is *illegal*
according to current (and draft) ANSI/ISO Standards.  Some compilers DO
allow this (as an extension) but not all compilers even agree on the
semantics when it is allowed.

I believe that MOST compilers that do allow nested ODO's do require that the
"object of the ODO" be in fixed storage - either in the fixed part of a
record or in another part of storage (such as working-storage).

I think the recommendation that I would make (agreeing with at least one
other already posted) is to NOT try an do this with nested variable length
tables.  Rather use fixed length tables - but with a "counter" field that
tells how many entries are "useful" and where "empty" entries begin - at
each level of the table.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Write variable length records....

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T06:43:59+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CBA75FB.5923895@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<26282372.0204141041.70dfc8b6@posting.google.com> <a9dk8c$6hk$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`

```


"William M. Klein" wrote:

> Others have already asked which compiler you are using.  Having an Occurs
> Depending ON *nested* within another ODO (Occurs Depending On) is *illegal*
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> each level of the table.

To add to what Bill says above, having sent my message, I then realized (Oops !)
you were asking about "WRITING ...". As Richard says **DON'T**.  As I
anticipated you might come back and ask, I tried to write your table to a file -
what a NIGHTMARE - I gave up !!!

It's  OK to use this varying approach for tables in memory, but it doesn't
really gain you anything. Specify a table up to a maximum of say 3,000
characters and whether or not you actually use the full table, memory is still
allocated. (Ignore the 'varying' and just use a counter for elements in the
table which are currently active - reiterating what Bill says above).

It's a different ball game with OO collections/dictionaries where you only
absorb the memory you actually need for the elements included in  your
collection(table). Add new elements  and your collection size increases; delete
elements - no change, not until you tell the collection to resize itself.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: Write variable length records....

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T16:06:29+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CBAEC75.E01E723D@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<26282372.0204141041.70dfc8b6@posting.google.com>`

```
Mikey wrote:

> Any suggestion how to implement a 2 level variable record ?

The short answer is "don't".  The long answer rants on about proper data
analysis, normalisation, and how it would be done in a sane wnvironment,
the conclusion of which is "don't".

Variable records because of occurs depending on may have been required
when there was a limit to the number of tape decks available for writing
data, but in modern systems (such as the last quarter century), there
should be no excuse for not structuring the data properly in separate
files.
```

<a id="t-3f5509714e"></a>
## Thread: call to printer in Personal COBOL for Win — 2 msgs

### call to printer in Personal COBOL for Win

- **From:** "PamelaRose" <prose@splatzone.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-14T22:07:26+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<y4nu8.893$xb.457783@monger.newsread.com>`

```
I found this on the help CD
Hope it helps  : )

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. COprint.
      *****************************************************************
      *
      *  This program will read student's names and grades from a
      *  file. The program will output the student's names and the
      *  their average grades.
      *
      *****************************************************************
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
           SELECT STUDENT-FILE
               ASSIGN TO DISK-INPUT-FILE
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
           SELECT TRANSCRIPT-FILE
               ASSIGN TO "outrec"
      *****************************************************************
      *
      * The ASSIGN statement for TRANSCRIPT-FILE was changed to a
      * file so that we could use the PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT library
      * routine. The library explaination is below.
      *
      *****************************************************************
               ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL.
      *****************************************************************
      *
      * The ORGANIZATION IS LINE SEQUENTIAL is the statement that
      * allows printing in line-over-line, instead of one long one.
      *
      *****************************************************************
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       FD  STUDENT-FILE
           LABEL RECORDS ARE STANDARD.
       01  STUDENT-REC.
           05  NAME-IN                 PIC X(20).
           05  GRADE1                  PIC 999.
           05  GRADE2                  PIC 999.
           05  GRADE3                  PIC 999.
       FD  TRANSCRIPT-FILE
           LABEL RECORDS ARE OMITTED.
       01  TRANSCRIPT-REC.
           05  NAME-OUT                PIC X(20).
           05  AVERAGE                 PIC 999.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       01  DISK-INPUT-FILE        PIC X(12)  VALUE "DATAFILE.DAT".
       01  ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS PIC X(3)   VALUE "YES".
      * ***************************************************************
      *
      * The procedure division is divided into two modules. The 100-
      * MAIN-MODLUE will perform until there are no more records.
      * The 200-CALC-RTN will calulate the average grades and write
      * out names and average grades.
      *
      *****************************************************************
       01  MFPRNT16PTR PROCEDURE-POINTER.
      *****************************************************************
      *
      * The PROCEDURE-POINTER above was added to stop the possibility
      * of a printer error caused by the PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT, that
      * library routine is part of the MFPRNT16.DLL and is not
      * callable without the PROCEDURE-POINTER because there is not
      * a valid entry point for COBOL to locate, causing an error 173
      *
      *****************************************************************
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
           SET MFPRNT16PTR TO ENTRY 'MFPRNT16'.
      *****************************************************************
      *
      * The setting of the PROCEDURE-POINTER. (above)
      *
      *****************************************************************
       100-MAIN-MODULE.
           OPEN INPUT STUDENT-FILE
                OUTPUT  TRANSCRIPT-FILE
           PERFORM UNTIL ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS = 'NO '
               READ STUDENT-FILE
                   AT END
                       MOVE 'NO ' TO ARE-THERE-MORE-RECORDS
                   NOT AT END
                       PERFORM 200-CALC-RTN
                   END-READ
           END-PERFORM
           MOVE x"0C" to TRANSCRIPT-REC
      *****************************************************************
      *
      * Above is a hard code HEX FORM FEED, for an explaination of
      * hard code HEX codes see your documentation or external
      * references to HEX codes.
      *
      *****************************************************************
           WRITE TRANSCRIPT-REC
           .

           CLOSE   STUDENT-FILE
                   TRANSCRIPT-FILE
           PERFORM 300-PRINT-FILE
           STOP RUN.
       200-CALC-RTN.
           MOVE NAME-IN TO NAME-OUT
           ADD GRADE1, GRADE2, GRADE3
               GIVING AVERAGE
           DIVIDE 3 INTO AVERAGE
           WRITE TRANSCRIPT-REC
           .
       300-PRINT-FILE.
            call "PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT"
                      using by reference "outrec" &x"00"
                            by reference "Output Record" &x"00"
                      BY VALUE 4 SIZE 2.
           .
      *****************************************************************
      *
      * PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT
      *
      * Sends the specified file to the printer, giving it a document
      * name and displaying the printer-options dialog box.
      *
      *
      * call "PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT" using     filename
      *                                        doc-name
      *                               by value options size 2
      *                              returning status-code
      *
      * Parameters:
      *
      * filename    pic x(<n>).
      * doc-name    pic x(<m>).
      * options    numeric literal.
      * status-code    See Key
      *
      * On Entry:
      *
      * filename    The name of the file to be printed. The name must
      * be space- or null-terminated.
      *
      * doc-name    The document name to be displayed. The name must
      * be null-terminated.
      *
      * options    A set of bits to set printer options:
      *
      * bit 3    Print in landscape orientation
      * bit 2    Print in portrait orientation
      * bit 1    Display a font dialog box, so you can select the
      *          default font for the document
      * bit 0    Display a printer control dialog box, so you can
      *          set up the printer
      *
      *   CONVERT BIT TO VALUES KEY
      *        BIT 0 = Value 1
      *        BIT 1 = Value 2
      *        BIT 2 = Value 4
      *        BIT 3 = Value 8
      *
      * Bits 2 and 3 cannot be used with bit 0 set.
      *
      *
      * On Exit:
      *
      * status-code    Printer status code:
      *
      * 0    Successful
      * 1    Could not open printer device
      * 4    Out of memory while printing
      * 5    Failed to open file
      * 6    Disk full while spooling file
      * 7    Print job aborted, no file spooled to Print Manager
      * 8    Printer information structure badly constructed
      * 9    No default printer found. Select a printer from Windows
      *      Control Panel.
      * 10    No commdlg.dll found. Should be in Windows system
      *       directory.
      * 11    Write failure
      *
      * Comments:
      *
      *
      * This routine is available only in the Windows environment.
      *
      * Example:
      *
      * To print out the file c:\config.sys in landscape:
      *
      *
      *      call "PC_WIN_PRINT_FILE_EXT"
      *                using by reference "c:\config.sys" &x"00"
      *                      by reference "My Config.sys" &x"00"
      *                      by value 8 size 2
      *                 The hard code HEX x"00" is a NULL.
      *
      *****************************************************************
```

<a id="t-98bfd68f07"></a>
## Thread: Justify left numeric field, VAX/VMS Cobol — 6 msgs

### Justify left numeric field, VAX/VMS Cobol

- **From:** "Georgi Kozinakov" <gjokok@yahoo.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T18:16:36+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3cbafbc8@news.mt.net.mk>`

```
Hi,

Anybody know if there is a simple way (with intrinsic function or
using justify clauses etc) to justify left a numeric field in a report.
For ex:

num-field pic 9(6).
rep-field pic Z(5)9.

move num-field to rep-field.

this gives:
num-field    rep-field
25                "    25"

and I want:    "25    "
```

#### ↳ Re: Justify left numeric field, VAX/VMS Cobol

- **From:** "Ron" <Ron@NoSpamForMe.Com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T14:51:08-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9favf$k3m$1@suaar1ab.prod.compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<3cbafbc8@news.mt.net.mk>`

```
I usually do something like:

05 tc pic 99.  (tally counter)

05 num-field pic 9(6) value 25.
05 rep-field pic z(5)9.
05 rep-field-x redefines rep-field pic x(6).
05 left-field pic x(6).

move num-field to rep-field.
move zero to tc.
inspect rep-field-x tallying tc for leading spaces.
move rep-field-x (tc + 1 : length of rep-field-x - tc) to left-field.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Justify left numeric field, VAX/VMS Cobol

- **From:** jnjsle@aol.com (Jnjsle)
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T21:55:39+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020415175539.22918.00003347@mb-cn.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<a9favf$k3m$1@suaar1ab.prod.compuserve.com>`

```
It would be nice if COBOL could solve this before, what the doom sayers claim
to be, its imminent demise.  It’s only been close to fifty years.

Here are the possibilities:
 [code]
            ‘bbb25’   ===>  ‘25bbb‘        justify left with space fill
       
            ‘bbb25’   ===>  ‘25000‘        justify left with zeros fill
       
            ‘00025’  ===>  ‘25000‘        justify left with zeros fill 
      
            ‘00025’  ===>  ‘25bbb‘        justify left with space fill 
      

            ‘25bbb’  ===>  ‘bbb25‘        justify right with space fill
       
            ‘25bbb’  ===>  ‘00025‘        justify right with zeros fill
       
            ‘25000’ ===>  ‘00025‘        justify right with zeros fill 
      
            ‘25000’ ===>  ‘bbb25‘        justify right with space fill 
      

How about an intrinsic function like:

MOVE FUNCTION JUST-LEFT-W-TRAILING-SPACES     (fld-a)   TO just-fld
MOVE FUNCTION JUST-LEFT-W-TRAILING-ZEROS       (fld-a)   TO just-fld

MOVE FUNCTION JUST-RIGHT-W-LEADING-SPACES   (fld-a)   TO just-fld
MOVE FUNCTION JUST-RIGHT-W-LEADING-ZEROS     (fld-a)   TO just-fld
[/code]
If I wind up w/alphas in a numeric field, that’s my problem, I’ll deal with
it. Just don’t quit on me.  While routines like those provided by Ron get the
job done (and we all use them), they clutter up the code distract the reader
from the "business" logic, one of  the primary goals of coBol.

Well, that’s my .02, Jack.  


Jack
```

## Thread: some problems with this prog.. :(( — 4 msgs

### some problems with this prog.. :((

- **From:** "Tomba" <tombatore@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T22:58:48+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3cbb3f04$0$6955$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
hy,
this is a small thingy that I made to start the whole prog. (MS cobol 4.5)
however, I have a few questions about it,
I hope you all can help me a way (this is start.cbl)

IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
AUTHOR. Steven De Groote.
PROGRAM-ID. startscherm is initial.
ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
CONFIGURATION SECTION.
special-names.
crt status is key-status.

DATA DIVISION.
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
01 set-bit-pairs pic 9(2) comp-x value 1.
01 user-key-control.
04 enable-fn-keys pic 9(2) comp-x value 1.
04 filler pic x value "1".
04 first-user-key pic 9(2) comp-x value 1.
04 number-of-keys pic 9(2) comp-x value 10.
01 key-status.
04 toets.
88 f1-key value x"3101".
88 f2-key value x"3102".
88 f3-key value x"3103".
88 f4-key value x"3104".
88 end-key value x"320a".
08 key-type pic x.
08 key-code-1 pic 9(2) comp-x.
04 key-code-2 pic 9(2) comp-x.

SCREEN SECTION.
copy "START.ss".
copy "BACKTOM.ss".
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
begin.
perform with test after until end-key
display backtom
display g-start
call x"af" using set-bit-pairs
accept toets
perform verwerk-toets
end-perform
goback.
verwerk-toets.
call x"af" using set-bit-pairs
user-key-control
evaluate true
when f1-key continue
when f2-key continue
when f3-key continue
when f4-key continue
when other continue
end-evaluate.

1. when I compile this, it says I should use nestall. it's solved by
removing the program-id. this isn't nested !! :-S
2. my end-key does not stop the program.. what is wrong?
3. I have that cursor blinking on the screen. How can I remove that?

hope you all can help me a little
I'm still learning it all ...

greets,

  Tomba
```

#### ↳ Re: some problems with this prog.. :((

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T16:19:08-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9fg4m$a4v$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<3cbb3f04$0$6955$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
The "quick" thing that I can see on WHY it wants NESTCALL, is that you have
the PROGRAM-ID paragraph *after* the AUTHOR paragraph, instead of before it.
Change the order of those two paragraphs and you won't need NESTCALL.
```

#### ↳ Re: some problems with this prog.. :((

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@invalid.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T22:01:40+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<85Iu8.40759$%l3.3923941@bin8.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<3cbb3f04$0$6955$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```

"Tomba" <tombatore@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:3cbb3f04$0$6955$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be...
> hy,
> this is a small thingy that I made to start the whole prog. (MS cobol 4.5)
…[57 more quoted lines elided]…
> 3. I have that cursor blinking on the screen. How can I remove that?

(Mostly) Standard Practice.
1. Cultivate the habit of confining PROGRAM-ID names to eight characters or
less.
2. Leave out the AUTHOR paragraph. This is included in the shop-standard
comment section.
3. Use copybook extensions of .COB or .CBL.
```

#### ↳ Re: some problems with this prog.. :((

- **From:** stephenjspiro@mail.com (Stephen J Spiro)
- **Date:** 2002-04-15T21:32:16-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<928495c6.0204152032.3cb4e359@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3cbb3f04$0$6955$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
Isn't that nice new "free form" format just SO pretty.  Makes COBOL
just SO easy to follow and understand, just like C.


I consistently agitated for the return of SECTION A and SECTION B, in
J4, and was consistently voted down.

I recommend to my clients that they prohibit their staff from using
"free form".
 
What is really funny, is that all the examples of "free form" in the
standard use indenting, and the other elements of "prettyprint"!  I
would fire a programmer who wrote code like this.  I will give Tomba
the benefit of the doubt, and assume that his actual work is more
readable than this.


Stephen J Spiro
Member, J4 COBOL Standards Committee


> IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
> AUTHOR. Steven De Groote.
…[48 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

Sean O'Flaherty was the biggest, brawniest bugger yers ever met, and took all the
weight lifting prizes at the local fairs. Having had a few jars one night, and
feelin' a bit unsteady, decided to walk home direct from the pub cutting across the
bog country.

Stumblin' through the gloom he hears a plaintiff cry. "Help me! Help me !". He
spots a wee leprechaun of a man stuck in the muddy bog up to his neck, just his
face and bowler showing, and sure as hell slowly receding into the depths. "Can I
help you surr ?",  says Sean. "Indeed you can. Oi'd be so grateful", says Michael
O'Rourke, who is stuck in the mud.

Without further ado,  Sean brings his mighty strength into play, huggin' and
heavin' at the pitiless creature of a man stuck in the bog. He's sweating gobs
doing it, losing the benefit of the eight Guinness he had before leavin' the pub.
It's no good - the little sod  is stuck, Sean just can't get him out. He gives up
in despair.

The pitiable little fella offers a suggestion to Sean. "Do yer think it moight help
if Oi took me feet out of the stirrups ?".

PS: Where can I get a green card ?  I could handle $55K US without too much trouble
<G>.

Jimmy

<a id="t-23e8e8f00f"></a>
## Thread: ASCII to EBCIDIC to COBOL COMP field — 1 msgs

### ASCII to EBCIDIC to COBOL COMP field

- **From:** jnjsle@aol.com (Jnjsle)
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T04:03:29+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20020416000329.22914.00003328@mb-cn.aol.com>`

```
Hi Luke,

If I read you correctly, you want to move the string '0689' to the COMP field
IN-BUFFER-SIZE.

Here are 2 possible approaches:

MOVE X'0689' TO IN-BUFFER-SIZE(1:2)  This I haven't tried, but should work.
                                                             When a field is
reference modified, it's
                                                             treated as
alphanumeric, regardless of
                                                             its original class
(in this case, numeric).


This one I've used before and know it works:
 
05  IN-BUFFER-SIZE    PIC 9(4) COMP.
05  IN-BUFFER-SIZE-X REDEFINES   
     IN-BUFFER-SIZE    PIC X(2).

MOVE X'0689' TO IN-BUFFER-SIZE-X

Just to make sure we understand each other, if you got a hex dump of your pgm
after the move, IN-BUFFER-SIZE would contain '0689'. The decimal equivilant
would be '1673'.
```

---

<a id="t-2e181bef48"></a>
## Thread: dynamic SQL — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### dynamic SQL

- **From:** "Maurizio Giacobbe" <maug7@yahoo.it>
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T06:10:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<46d5cffe44fa0ea5c481b5023cb60e26.69693@mygate.mailgate.org>`

```
Who can help me with the instruction of dynamic SQL with NetExpress 3.1
? i need one example of program to see how it works.

Thank
```

#### ↳ Re: dynamic SQL

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T06:43:42+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CBBC782.999FC9D7@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<46d5cffe44fa0ea5c481b5023cb60e26.69693@mygate.mailgate.org>`

```


Maurizio Giacobbe wrote:

> Who can help me with the instruction of dynamic SQL with NetExpress 3.1
> ? i need one example of program to see how it works.
>
> Thank
>

Maurizio,

I'll send you an example tomorrow. Meanwhile, did you get a copy of Will
(Wilson) Price's book "Elements of COBOL Web Programming with M/F N/E",
when you received your N/E package for V 3.1 ?  If you go to Page  295 -
Relational Databases and SQL - he gives you a pretty good intro to SQL. He
sticks to the basics, no reference to Normalization etc.

N/E's own on-line help book about SQL and DBs is a damned good reference
too - and again spells out ESQL Assistant.

At first glance it looks complicated, but when you get into ESQL Assistant
and get the hang of clicking on the appropriate buttons/icons, (there are
only THREE !) it does a LOT of work for you,  creating your copy books for
host, cobol and null variables, plus formatting your queries which you can
auto paste into your source code..

If you don't have Will's book, look at the N/E demos, they provide tables
set up using MS Access which you can use.

There are some sites you will find useful for general SQL syntax,
regardless of programming language. Although I've downloaded hard copies I
didn't make a note of the sites. (Do a web search using Web Ferret or
Google on "ESQL" or "SQL"). This one is interesting - the author is a movie
nut !

            http://www.fluffycat.com/sql/

Until tomorrow.

Jimmy Calgary AB
```

## Thread: COBOL and report writers vs generators — 4 msgs

### COBOL and report writers vs generators

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T20:40:06-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a9ijq4$d76$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I know that this group often discusses COBOL's "built-in" Report Writer vs
"procedural report logic" vs add-on report generator products.  Given all of
this, I though "you all" might be interested in one of IBM's announcements
today.  See:

 http://www.ibmlink.ibm.com/usalets&parms=H_202-094

For a tool to "migrate" from a report generator to Standard COBOL source
code !!!
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL and report writers vs generators

- **From:** "Leahy, Don" <zzdlf@tdbank.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-17T10:33:11-04:00

- **References:** `<a9ijq4$d76$1@nntp9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
This must be part of IBM's strategy to reduce the barriers to mainframe
ownership by offering customers alternatives to 3rd party software products.
In most mainframe shops, the cost of 3rd party (ie, non-IBM) software makes
up a large percentage of their total operating costs.  IBM has recently
started to offer products that compete directly with FileAid, AbendAid and
Expediter, and now they're going after Easytrieve/Plus.

<a id="t-9acc1f6584"></a>
## Thread: Opinion needed of .NET and COBOL.net — 14 msgs

_Topics: [`Web, XML, modern integration`](topics.md#web)_

### Opinion needed of .NET and COBOL.net

- **From:** maross@texoma.net (Mike)
- **Date:** 2002-04-16T20:25:11-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2763b81a.0204161925.58ef9217@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi. I' am trying to evaluate my possible upgrade to Fujitsu's COBOL
for .NET framework. I am Upgrading my pc COBOL programs to be web
enabled for a larger audience as well as make updates easier(place
Http links for instant updates on server). However since learning
DHTML (JavaScript/CSS/html) plus server CGI (Perl/COBOL-CGI/Java), I
find COBOL.NET or C#(however better than COBOL.NET) that it takes
twice as much code, learning and is non transportable compared to the
new features in the HTML/javascript-6 level browsers (ie6/ns6.2). In
fact looking at www.Cobolscript.com as a CGI that is transportable to
Linux/windows/Sun and works with browsers for $49 make more sense than
Fujitsu's $4200 package; however there seems to be no user
base/threads in CobolScript and the debugger lacks Fujitsu's well
developed environment and exe-CGI. Fujitsu NET also gives you free
VB.net, which by the threads of VB6 users looks like it is better to
just learn C# and give the  last rights to VB6 (and maybe Powercobol
as this is basically VB6). My only reservations to anything with
JavaScript are that everyone can see most of your code, which makes
CGI.exe's calls needed. I've seen so many COBOL display-accept schemes
since my first MS-2.0 COBOL compiler in1984 that using
HTML/JavaScript-streaming-Multimedia interpreters (MS.NET's CLR and
Java are also interpreted) is what the user wants verses second rate
COBOL text/semi-multimedia solutions.
Note here is COBOL.net reference to html text/scr(img)  from
http://www.adtools.com/info/whitepaper/net.html
<html><head><script runat="server" language="COBOL"> 
ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
CONFIGURATION SECTION.
REPOSITORY.
PROPERTY SELECT-VALUE AS "Value"
PROPERTY IMAGE-SRC AS "Src"                    
PROPERTY SPAN-VALUE AS "InnerHtml"
CLASS STRING-BUILDER AS "System.Text.StringBuilder"
CLASS SYS-STRING AS "System.String"
CLASS SYS-OBJECT AS "System.Object"
CLASS EVENTARGS AS "System.EventArgs".
OBJECT.
METHOD-ID. FRUITLIST-CLICK.
DATA DIVISION.
WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
77 IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER OBJECT REFERENCE STRING-BUILDER.
77 IMAGE-FILE-STR OBJECT REFERENCE SYS-STRING.
LINKAGE SECTION.
77 EVENT-SOURCE OBJECT REFERENCE SYS-OBJECT.
77 EVENT OBJECT REFERENCE EVENTARGS.
PROCEDURE DIVISION USING BY VALUE EVENT-SOURCE EVENT. 
   MOVE SELECT-VALUE OF FRUITLIST TO SPAN-VALUE OF FRUITNAME.
   INVOKE STRING-BUILDER "NEW" USING BY VALUE "images/"
   RETURNING IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER.
   INVOKE IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER "Append"
          USING BY VALUE SELECT-VALUE OF FRUITLIST
                RETURNING IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER.
   INVOKE IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER "Append" USING BY VALUE ".jpg"
                RETURNING IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER.
   INVOKE IMAGE-FILE-BUILDER "ToString"
                RETURNING IMAGE-FILE-STR.
   MOVE IMAGE-FILE-STR TO IMAGE-SRC OF FRUITIMAGE.
   END METHOD FRUITLIST-CLICK.
   END OBJECT. 
</script> 
</head>
<body>
<form runat="server"> 
<font face="Verdana">
<b>Please Select A Fruit : </b>
<select id="FRUITLIST" runat="server" size="1"> 
<option value="Orange">Orange</option>
<option value="Apple">Apple</option>
<option value="Mango">Mango</option>
<option value="Pear">Pear</option>
<option value="Banana">Banana</option>
</select>
<input type="submit" value="submit" runat="server"
OnServerClick="FRUITLIST-CLICK">
<p><table><tr><td><img id="FRUITIMAGE" src="images\blank.gif"
runat="server" /></td>
              <td><font face="Verdana" size=6><span id="FRUITNAME"
runat="server"/></font></td></tr>
   </table></font>
</form></body></html>
************* End COBOL/html program *****Start JavaScript solution
THAT DOES SAME AS ABOVE **
<html><head>
<script  language="javascript"> 
function fruitlistClick(funcVarible)                                  
                            {
if (funcVarible  == "man50kg.jpg") 
    {theImg  = document.getElementById("fruitImage").setAttribute("src","man50kg.jpg");}
//note1**  above statement evaluates, swaps, DOC model vs NetCOBOLs 
if (funcVarible  == "man70kg.jpg")
    {theImg  = document.getElementById("fruitImage").setAttribute("src","man70kg.jpg");}
alert(funcVarible);                                                   
                             }
</script> </head><body>
<form>
   <b>Please Select A Fruit : </b>
   <select id="fruitList" size="1" value="Orange"
onChange="fruitlistClick(form.fruitList.value)" >
   <option value="man50kg.jpg">Orange man</option>
   <option value="man70kg.jpg">Apple man</option>
   </select><p><table><tr><td><img id="fruitImage" src="man50kg.jpg"
height="350" width="230"></td></table>
</form></body></html>
**** End JavaScript to work change all *.jpgs to YOUR  images ***
please look at above note1** that replaces all class/property/invokes
in NetCOBOL **********
Please note you must address the CLR API Class and set the properties
prior to settings a variable. Which means you must double define all
variables as you are also addressing each class/property of the CLR,
this only creates a need for programmers to understand the CLR or Java
API call structures and properties, and if you study java/C#.net why
not just drop COBOL?
IMHO this MS.NET seems to be an attempt to trap developers into
proprietary MS-C#/MS-OS technology to offset the
LINUX/Netscape6.2/java revenue bleed to supplement Gates bad
investment's in Telecom/web and etc crap at the expense of their new
6.0 upgrade model for enterprises. Just wait to the 7.0 upgrade .NET
model gets them. Plus Java/JavaScript advice and sample programs can
easily be found on the WWW.
  Sorry for the long rant, but these upgrades that claim vast
technology shifts leads to 100s of maintenance hours for me and adds
TCO to us and our customers along with decreased productivity.  Thank
you Mike.
```

#### ↳ Re: Opinion needed of .NET and COBOL.net

I am NOT saying that Fujitsu isn't on the right path - see for example
 http://www.fawcette.com/dotnetmag/2001_12/online/online_eprods/bmeyer/
    or
 http://www.fawcette.com/dotnetmag/2001_12/online/online_eprods/bmeyer/

On the other hand, if you want to read a DIFFERENT evaluation of COBOL and
.NET, see the Micro Focus' paper at:

   http://www.microfocus.com/files/whitepapers/netwp.pdf

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Opinion needed of .NET and COBOL.net

- **From:** usenetrob@hotmail.com (Rob)
- **Date:** 2002-04-17T20:01:05-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3826a0a6.0204171901.336005b5@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<2763b81a.0204161925.58ef9217@posting.google.com> <a9isb2$at8$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
> On the other hand, if you want to read a DIFFERENT evaluation of COBOL and
> .NET, see the Micro Focus' paper at:
> 
>    http://www.microfocus.com/files/whitepapers/netwp.pdf

This seems to push the Interop with COM components, which is slower.
This article is also pretty old (Beta 2). DotNet has since gone
through a Release Candidate and a Release to Manufacturing.  Companies
who sell Interop as a long-term solution have chosen poorly, in my
opinion. I agree that it is necessary to use the Interop layer in the
meantime, but components will run much faster if re-written
specifically for .NET.
```

#### ↳ Re: Opinion needed of .NET and COBOL.net

- **From:** "Gianni Spano" <softline2000@tin.it>
- **Date:** 2002-04-17T10:09:59+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<6ef5cea0931b7776202a8157856c9e19.40368@mygate.mailgate.org>`
- **References:** `<2763b81a.0204161925.58ef9217@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello Mike

I'm Gianni Spano from Italy.
I'm a Fujitsu Powercobol user and i have had the same impressions when
Fujitsu announced .NET Cobol products.
Now i'm using Fujitsu Powercobol v6.1 and i'm planning to develop some
applications for the web.
Seeing the Fujitsu .Net preview i think it is so hard to learn, because
its new concepts and methods, and searching on the web i found
Cobolscript
(perhap i already know it).
Cobolscript is easy to use if you decide to develop applications for
the web, and has a low price, then i have decided to purchase a regular
license.
I think Cobolscript is a good starting point to develop continuing use
cobol, and sincerily i don't need to purchase Fujitsu .Net (for the
moment)
I will decide in the next future, seeing what it will happen.
Later this year Fujitsu will release its standard cobol v7.0 and
we will continue to use their environments to produce GUI applications,
hoping that it will have less bugs and incompatibilities  that v6.1 had.

Regards

Gianni

P.S. Sorry about my english, i hope you will understand what i want 
     to tell you.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Opinion needed of .NET and COBOL.net

- **From:** usenetrob@hotmail.com (Rob)
