Mark Dodel wrote:

> On Mon, 29 Apr 2002 16:08:06 UTC, "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
> wrote:
…[15 more quoted lines elided]…
> Its not just distasteful, it is illegal.<snip>...........

Don't get me wrong I'm not an ass licking MS advocate. More to the contrary, I'm deeply
disturbed at the concentration of options (power). (And regardless of what your judge
decreed, articles I read almost two years ago, forecast cynically, all that  Bill Gates
had to do was wait for a change in the Whie House).

I would have liked to have seen Torvald's Linux take off. Sure there are people
enthusiastically using it, but it is still largely 'an intersting approach'  in the
major part of the commercial world. Big problem, using that similar Unix command-line
syntax, it isn't exactly user-friendly. There was a glimmer of hope when Corel
announced they were going to introduce a 'Windows-like' front-end. But Copeland was so
enamoured of photo ops of his wife slipping out of the top of her dress, he lost it !

The only David that could possibly slay Goliath is Open Source. Well I'm sorry we have
activity going on in COBOL as I write, but at this juncture it just seems it is going
along like a nice academic exercise, albeit there's a small dedicated team putting in a
lot of effort. I believe their current objective is to be ANSI '85 compliant - that's
17 years ago ! It only makes sense to me when they are ANSI 2002 compliant  and that's
the complete kerboodle for OO.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Totally off topic Was: Kevin Li

_(thread depth: 12)_

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@invalid.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T21:33:28+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<I_iz8.371$q8.95047@bin3.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<3CCA6501.6B679005@shaw.ca> <3CCB9731.8E79C5A9@Azonic.co.nz> <3CCB0AD3.457868F5@shaw.ca> <ptKy8.7999$Q42.279404@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <aafvct$a6f$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-BKdkI4PmWzm1@localhost> <Xv1z8.253403$K5.20359643@bin5.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-veQe656Hnc52@localhost> <3CCCCF9B.531BDC10@shaw.ca> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-rwEhM5SRuzMC@localhost> <3CCD6F7D.3A0A5B6F@shaw.ca> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-zqVoXeoNu1Df@localhost>`

```

"Mark Dodel" <madodel@ptdprolog.net>
> -)
> -)Whether or not we consider the monopoly distasteful, Bill has done a
TREMENDOUS
> -)job with Windows, even if we feel locked in.
>
> Its not just distasteful, it is illegal.

What's illegal? Monopolies are not illegal.

>
> I am not locked in.  No microsoft virusware here at all. :-)
>
> -)
> -)Don't forget, his opportunity came about as the result of a succession
of events.
> -)
>
> Are you completely disavowing knowledge of the testimony at the latest
> sham trial known as the USDOJ vs. MSFT?  Many of those events were
> coecive and illegal.

And Microsoft was awarded penalty points.

>
> No monopoly is good, whether it be a government run/regulated one
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> others.

You are way off your meds, here.

1. Most large monopolies have been a benefit to the consumer. For example,
the prototypical monopoly, Standard Oil, drove down the cost of kerosene
from $3/gallon to less than 5 cents (simultaneously driving the whale-oil
industry out of business).

2. There is no such thing as a 'criminal' monopoly.

3. "Windoze (sic) is crap" Of course that's your judgement. The judgement of
a couple of hundred million others is at variance with yours. But they could
be wrong.

4. "...stolen or bought..." So what?

>
> Anything would have been better then the bloated, mediocre drivel and
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> in COBOL.

In addition to being off your meds, you've evidently taken too much adult
beverage.

1. "Anything would have been better..." is nonsense. We had other things -
DOS, PC-DOS, OS/2, Unix, etc. The market spoke.

2. "Clean, quality code that acutally works.." happens every day: a trillion
email messages, most composed or read with Outlook. Millions and millions of
letters, spread-sheets, and databases created or accessed, most with Office.
Hundreds of millions of computers boot up and work flawlessly with Windows.
Every day.

3. "All in the name of windoze (sic) everywhere" Windows everywhere is good.
Standardization is good (like the power distribution network, colors on stop
lights, accounting methods, etc.). In our case, we used to have rooms full
of printers and modems to test our software with every concievable
combination. Now we hand that whole sorry mess over to Windows and
concentrate on our application.

4. "... the mess we are in today..." is an admirable mess. We - as a nation
and a computing society - have the highest productivity imaginable.

5. "... kiss everything but microslop (sic) goodbye..." I certainly hope so.
I dream of the day when the lunatic-fringe players give up and disappear.

>
> Whew, boy you should never have gotten me started.  ;-)
>

Hating Microsoft is not good for you - each day it becomes harder and harder
to find justifications. So, just relax. Go with the flow. You, too, can
become a pod person. It is a wonderfully liberating experience! Peace. Joy.
Fulfillment. Just close your eyes and accept it. You're drifting off now.
When you awake.....
```

http://www.antitrustupdate.com/Statutes/ShermanAct/ST-Sherman1-4.html#
2

"15 U.S.C. 2: Monopolizing trade a felony; penalty

Every person who shall monopolize, or attempt to monopolize, 
or combine or conspire with any other person or persons,
to monopolize any part of the trade or commerce among the 
several States, or with foreign nations, shall be deemed guilty 
of a felony, and, on conviction thereof, shall be punished by
fine not exceeding $10,000,000 if a corporation, or, if any other 
person, $350,000, or by imprisonment not exceeding three years,
or by both said punishments, in the discretion of the court."

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Totally off topic Was: Kevin Li

_(thread depth: 11)_

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T07:52:03+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CCE3F13.64C64B69@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<3CCA6501.6B679005@shaw.ca> <3CCB9731.8E79C5A9@Azonic.co.nz> <3CCB0AD3.457868F5@shaw.ca> <ptKy8.7999$Q42.279404@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <aafvct$a6f$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-BKdkI4PmWzm1@localhost> <Xv1z8.253403$K5.20359643@bin5.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-veQe656Hnc52@localhost> <3CCCCF9B.531BDC10@shaw.ca> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-rwEhM5SRuzMC@localhost> <3CCD6F7D.3A0A5B6F@shaw.ca>`

```
James J. Gavan wrote:
> 
> Whether or not we consider the monopoly distasteful, Bill has done a TREMENDOUS
> job with Windows, even if we feel locked in.

No more and no less than any other company or companies could have done
with their own products if they hadn't been squashed like bugs.

> IBM's first choice was Gary (????? who wrote CP/M ?????). As the tale goes, two
> IBM suits arranged to meet him, and he left them fuming on the ground while he
> whistled around in his airplane above on a jaunt. 

Complete myth.  DRI's contract negotiator met with the IBM team, as was
appropriate.  Gary's skills weren't in dealing with people so he
delegated that, as is often the case.  The main issue was that IBM
wanted a completely one sided non-disclosure agreement that the DRI
Lawyer did not approve signing of.

> Don't knock poor Gerstner, (well he aint really 'poor' is he ?). Whereas you are
> a 'loyalist' to OS/2, IBM had to become 'realists' - OS/2 was going nowhere.

MS's 'per box' licensing made sure of that.

> It bears thinking about. If Bill Gates hadn't opened up the world with Windows,
> due to a contract restriction, would Big Blue, somewhat of a pondering giant,
> have gotten us to where we are today ? 

IBM was 'pondering' mainly because it was operating under a consent
decree that prevented it from announcing products before they were
actually available, and gasp horror, they actually worked within their
agreement instead of completely ignoring consents.

The result was that IBM had to advertise currently available products
while MS hyped up vapourware that were years in the future (but promised
for real soon now) and sometimes never appeared at all (see Cairo). 
This gave the impression that MS were far more advanced while they
served up DOS based systems (and said they weren't).

> Consider their approach to OO COBOL, they
> keep shuffling it like a pack of cards.

Didn't this have something to do with the fact that the standards are
_still_ not set.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Totally off topic Was: Kevin Li

_(thread depth: 12)_

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T22:25:05+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CCDC7CB.1C7AE60C@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<3CCA6501.6B679005@shaw.ca> <3CCB9731.8E79C5A9@Azonic.co.nz> <3CCB0AD3.457868F5@shaw.ca> <ptKy8.7999$Q42.279404@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <aafvct$a6f$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-BKdkI4PmWzm1@localhost> <Xv1z8.253403$K5.20359643@bin5.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-veQe656Hnc52@localhost> <3CCCCF9B.531BDC10@shaw.ca> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-rwEhM5SRuzMC@localhost> <3CCD6F7D.3A0A5B6F@shaw.ca> <3CCE3F13.64C64B69@Azonic.co.nz>`

```


Richard Plinston wrote:

I'd forgotten you would have more than a passing interest in something written about
MicroSloth <G>

>
> IBM was 'pondering' mainly because it was operating under a consent
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

While not myth, that isn't totally accurate either. I think you were at ICT in UK
before you went to NZ. Unigate, we had the ICT 1500 with the RCA MCF. Now I can't
guarantee the model number, could it be a very early version of IBM 360 (?) with the
very first hard disks. The word we kept getting back was they didn't work, (the
disks). ICT gleefully followed up and made additional sales based on IBM's failures.
(Yes we *know* they worked eventually - but a case of marketing outstripping
technology, before proven)..

I'm sure I've mentioned from Debenhams days, mid 60's. We took a look at OCR/OMR. IBM
had an OCR machine, input forms contained those typical light blue boxes and carefully
hand write a number into. We were given the sales hype, minus the lunch and booze
ICT/ICL used to throw in at Putney Bridge <G>. The IBM version was stick your sixpence
into a vending machine and get something back which was semi warm and supposed to be
coffee !

Not unreasonably I asked if I could take back a pile of forms to get different samples
of hand-writing. The salesman agreed without equivocation. Got back, did a simple one
page instruction sheet and circulated to our some 300 accounting staff.
Forms completed, duly mailed off to IBM. Never heard from them again !!!!!

>
> > Consider their approach to OO COBOL, they
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> _still_ not set.

Yeah.... BUT they have *always* been on the J4 (ANSI) team putting in their two cents.
(Dual role; same lady chairs WG4 (ISO)). They have *never* been non participants. And
frankly what may be coming back from their J4 representative is not necessarily what
the boys in suits implement. Case in point, Screen Section - they aren't going to
implement. As regards OO they went the SOM route - now currently, we've got some
minimal OO classes, purely to link you to Java, as Bill says *providing* you upgrade
the compiler, (The Triumvirate, IBM, Sun and HP v. MS.).

As to the standard 'not yet being set', OO COBOL has been touted since at least '93.
It is very firm in COBOL 2002, and as a user, I would be very, very surprised if
something innovative was added at some future date. I'm talking about the *basics* for
OO not things like GUIs, Web etc., which have importance primarily to us "desktop"
people. With an aversion to acknowledging any OS the Standard will probably never have
anything to cover these features. Compare that to what I read about Java, the
capability of using the 'same' on different OS.

The more you think on it the Web is like a shifting sand dune - without doubt crazy to
try and keep a standard updated for every new gizmo that comes along. However if
something like an OO DB comes along, then this will very likely have impact. (And that
got mentioned in Ed Arranga's book - back in '96). But added to the standard, this
would be a new feature and shouldn't conflict with the basics I refer to above. Likely
such a module (OO DB), would "draw" resources as necessary from some of those basic
support classes.

The only gaping hole at the moment is lack of a Standard specification for
colections/dictionaries, and they are sitting down to look at this next month, May. A
big complex topic, and attempting to get it right first time, which is not what
happened with Java. The J-people finished up with workarounds. Completion date ? Who
knows ? (Just so long as this module isn't introduced as a  part of COBOL 2015. You
can wager by then I will be ga-ga or totally out to lunch <G>). Back to collections -
currently F/J, Hitachi and M/F have their own flavours. The last two take aim at the
Smalltalk approach.

Jimmy
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Totally off topic Was: Kevin Li

- **From:** maross@texoma.net (Mike)
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T09:25:24-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2763b81a.0204290825.6fd5cd0@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3CCA6501.6B679005@shaw.ca> <3CCB9731.8E79C5A9@Azonic.co.nz> <3CCB0AD3.457868F5@shaw.ca> <ptKy8.7999$Q42.279404@typhoon.austin.rr.com> <aafvct$a6f$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-BKdkI4PmWzm1@localhost> <Xv1z8.253403$K5.20359643@bin5.nnrp.aus1.giganews.com> <yRNWO1cJ1gUw-pn2-veQe656Hnc52@localhost> <3CCCCF9B.531BDC10@shaw.ca>`

```
Micro$oft has dumped their COBOL compiler years ago along with all
language standards. Now they want to allow all languages to work on
their Platform? However they are giving these compiler developers a
cut down version that does not include RDBMS functionality. What good
is Web Services with Flat or Indexed FILES? These cut down versions
are in NetCOBOL, C#, and VB standard. Micro$oft wants to sell everyone
Visual Studio .NET Enterprise and there SQL server. C# is a great Copy
of Delphi and Kylix (Linux) from the Borland programming genius Anders
Heijlsberg  http://www.gekko-software.nl/DotNet/Art00.htm We
programmers are going to MS like an addict to its dealer and hang
around them like jackals do with  lions hoping for scraps. Oddly MS is
in direct competition to every programmer. I am going to try
Macromedia's new Dreamweaver MX which allows true Web Services and a
off line application interface like VB with greater XML, RDBMS
functionality.
 Mike
```

##### ↳ ↳ Micro Focus OS/2 "support" status (was: Kevin Li

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-27T15:41:13-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aaf2db$cqp$1@slb0.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<3CCA6501.6B679005@shaw.ca> <3CCB9731.8E79C5A9@Azonic.co.nz>`

```
For the complete list of Micro Focus products which have "dropped" support -
or have ANNOUNCED dates for dropped support, see:

 http://www.microfocus.com/products/advisory/withdrawal.asp?bhcp=1

For both the "DOS, Windows and OS/2 Distributed Development Products" and
"DOS, Windows and OS/2 Mainframe Development Products" charts, the first
"note" says (slifhtly different for the 2 charts),

"Micro Focus Mainframe Express is only supported on 32-bit Windows systems
i.e. Windows 98 or later. Users of existing 16-bit or OS/2 products must
plan to migrate to a 32-bit Windows platform in order to upgrade to
Mainframe Express."

If you "click" on the frequently asked question link, you will see the
following question and answer,

"Until what date will Micro Focus support COBOL Workbench V4.0 on OS/2
platforms? Which OS/2 platforms are supported?
Support for all Micro Focus products released for use on OS/2 ended on June
1, 2001. Support for these products is no longer available."
```

- **From:** "Christine Geary" <cgeary@epix.net>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<BABy8.12932$r5.4534809@news1.epix.net>`

```
I would like to know how I can use Fujitsu COBOL with Windows ME.  I've
downloaded the program and I can't seem to get anything into sheet 1.  I
would like to know, therefore, how I can input data into sheet 1 (or any
other sheets for that matter).

Any help is appreciated; thanks in advance.

Christine Geary
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu COBOL 3.0 & Windows ME

- **From:** maross@texoma.net (Mike)
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T15:16:38-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2763b81a.0204301416.3caa22a@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<BABy8.12932$r5.4534809@news1.epix.net>`

```
Hi Christine,
  My Fujitsu 3.0 manual Requirements states Window 95/NT. I assume
your goal is self-teaching a COBOL GUI as nobody could be serious with
FSC 3.0 for a commercial product (only works on NT/95/3.1). I assumed
after file/new you right-click MainForm[form] and select open then
depress a control and left click? Or since you have the PowerCOBOL
interface up try opening up a PC 3.O project file.  However you may be
able to use the Fujitsu COBOL ANSI 85 features that are widely used in
backend systems today and port this to NETCOBOL or Fujitsu 6.0 later
with updated GUI's that have some merit. However NETCOBOL is including
MS Visual Basic NET free, which makes the next 5-year GUI lifespan of
PowerCOBOL murky at least.  If you do find an NT/95 OS note that
PowerCOBOL 4.0 versions and up syntax has changed dramatically.
 Mike
```

<a id="t-65f5346990"></a>
## Thread: IE from PowerCOBOL — 2 msgs

### IE from PowerCOBOL

- **From:** pcobolar@yahoo.com.ar (Jose)
- **Date:** 2002-04-27T20:53:41-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b8741b18.0204271953.7faf0e66@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello,
i found how to access a webpage from powercobol using IE without dde or API calls.
1) Add this code to the REPOSITORY of the form
     CLASS COM AS "*OLE".
2) Then add this code to the event you want
 WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
 01 IE-APP		PIC X(28) 	VALUE "InternetExplorer.APPLICATION".
 01 IE    		OBJECT REFERENCE COM.
 01 OLE-TRUE        	PIC 1 BIT  	VALUE B"1".
 01 url			PIC X(34)	VALUE "http://ar.geocities.com/pcobolar/".
 PROCEDURE       DIVISION.
     INVOKE COM	"CREATE-OBJECT"	USING	IE-APP	RETURNING	IE
     INVOKE IE 	"SET-Visible" 	USING 	OLE-TRUE
     INVOKE IE 	"NAVIGATE" 	USING 	BY REFERENCE url
     SET    IE     		TO    	NULL

and that's all.
Hope be usefull

Regards from Argentina

Jose Holzmann
http://ar.geocities.com/pcobolar/
```

```

## Thread: Help on Index file — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help on Index file

- **From:** markmak@netfront.net
- **Date:** 2002-04-28T10:05:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<mW.Gv9w0p.ru8@webnews.netfront.net>`

```
Dear all

I need your help.  My company buy a application packages developed by Micro 
Focus Cobol on DOS platform.  One day,  the Novell server was hang-up.  I used 
the "rebuild.exe" to recover DAT and IDX file.  The end-user used this 
application but the index was not right/correct because the sort order have 
some error.

For examples,  Using Pop-up menu to sort-out outstanding records but got some 
completed record.

Anybody to help me.  I am very very high appreciate for you help.

Thanks a lot!!!!!
```

#### ↳ Re: Help on Index file

- **From:** lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca
- **Date:** 2002-04-28T14:59:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-SsKBmGRyZ05R@thishost>`
- **References:** `<mW.Gv9w0p.ru8@webnews.netfront.net>`

```
On Sun, 28 Apr 2002 10:05:13 UTC, markmak@netfront.net wrote:

> Dear all
> 
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks a lot!!!!!

Depending on the version of rebuild (and the file layout) you are 
using you could try the "/D" switch for the rebuild command.

rebuild originalFile.dat, newFile.dat /D

This will copy the original file to the new file and create an index 
by reading the data records only.

After it is finished , make a copy of the original .dat and .idx files
and copy the newFile .dat and .idx files to the original file name.
```

#### ↳ Re: Micro Focus cobol index file

- **From:** "Mike Kinasz" <mkinasz@cbspayroll.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T03:22:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<E%2z8.21089$ao1.8709@rwcrnsc54>`
- **References:** `<aagi6k$j8r$1@hfc.pacific.net.hk>`

```
Try this:  (assumes your isam file is FILE.DAT and FILE.IDX and REBUILD.EXE
located in same directory)

from a DOS prompt do this.  Change to the directory where your files are
located.  Then:

REN FILE.* OLD.*
REBUILD OLD.DAT,FILE.DAT /V/I/E/D

Ok....  try that FILE.DAT and FILE.IDX.    (that'll probably work)

If on the odd chance the problem still persists then try this.   Delete the
FILE.IDX and replace it with a FILE.IDX from a empty FILE.DAT (maybe you can
run your prog and it'll create a new FILE.DAT and FILE.IDX for you with no
records.... if so you can use that FILE.IDX along with the original FILE.DAT
and then repeat the above instructions (the ren and rebuild).

Hopefully that does the trick for ya,
Mike Kinasz

news:aagi6k$j8r$1@hfc.pacific.net.hk...

records

- **From:** "Poewes" <daggieken@pandora.be>
- **Date:** 2002-04-28T10:51:18+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<GuQy8.43687$Ze.7238@afrodite.telenet-ops.be>`

```
I have the folowing problem. I'm studying tables in Cobol (MF) at the moment
but i get an error I am not able to fix

It says my index (indexed by persindex) is not unique...

can anyone help me ? I c/p the problem below


WS
         01 Pers-tabel.
           03 Elem-pers occurs 20 indexed by persindex.
              05 i-dienst pic x(10).
                05 .....

        01 persindex PIC 9.
Procedure division
           perform until tabel-verwerkt
           search Elem-pers VARYING persindex
               at end set tabel-verwerkt to true
               when (i-loon(persindex) > 2500) and
                   (i-postc(persindex)=9300)
                    display ELEM-PERS(persindex) AT 0101
                    set gevonden to true
           end-search
           set persindex up by 1
           end-perform
```

#### ↳ Re: [newbe] index-table question

- **From:** marcel.brunner@gmx.net (Marcel Brunner)
- **Date:** 2002-04-28T07:55:09-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8798ba11.0204280655.6f48203f@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<GuQy8.43687$Ze.7238@afrodite.telenet-ops.be>`

```
"Poewes" <daggieken@pandora.be> wrote in message news:<GuQy8.43687$Ze.7238@afrodite.telenet-ops.be>...
> I have the folowing problem. I'm studying tables in Cobol (MF) at the moment
> but i get an error I am not able to fix
…[23 more quoted lines elided]…
>            end-perform

Hi Poewes
Using the statement "indexed by persindex" is
implicitly *creating* an index "persindex" - no need
to declare it separately in the WS.
So just leave away "01 persindex PIC 9." and your
program will work.

Regards,
Marcel
```

## Thread: Windows printing from COBOL — 8 this month / 19 total

### Windows printing from COBOL

- **From:** "Newsgroups" <thepla@mail.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-28T18:11:03-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ccc8231_2@corp-news.newsgroups.com>`

```
I currently use FORMPRINT and have been successful in creating reports. The
problem is that FORMPRINT generates one page at a time for viewing and the
user in view mode has to hit a button to view the next or previous screen,
one at a time. Even after all pages have been viewed once the user still
hits a button to go back and forth within the report. I want to be able to
crate a viewable report that works like preview mode in Quicken or
QuickBooks. A report where the user uses the bar on the right to scroll down
through the report. Does anybody have any ideas.

Thanks.
```

#### ↳ Re: Windows printing from COBOL

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T01:59:09+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CCCA881.6DEEDD09@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<3ccc8231_2@corp-news.newsgroups.com>`

```


Newsgroups wrote:

> I currently use FORMPRINT and have been successful in creating reports. The
> problem is that FORMPRINT generates one page at a time for viewing and the
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks.

I understand exactly where you are coming from. I use Quickbooks for the very
minimal bit of accounting that I need to do. That Print Preview is superb.
However something to bear in mind :-

    - Don't know what Quickbooks is written in - could be proprietary

    - That concept of holding one big data file - which in COBOL we would think
of
        as  both Transaction and Master files, (alternatively DBs accessed with
        SQL) -  most *obviously* they are using a DB - again might be
proprietary

    - Print Preview - purely a guess - but I bet very close to the truth - just
like
        COBOL Report Writer or Screen Section allow you to create print or
screen
        "masks", identifying the data sources for the masks - it just seems
logical to
        me that Quickbooks must function in a very similar fashion. (e.g. it
has  several
        masks for Balance Sheet presentation; you select different reporting
periods
        and in a flash of the eye it has retrieved and displayed the current
query).

Somebody using Micro Focus PC_PRINT routines did visit Newbury once, with a
suggestion that PC_PRINT  include Print Preview. It didn't happen, although the
print module is quite effective in other areas of printing. Other than going to
the depth that Intuit have with Quickbooks, and it is their customized software
that they are doing it for, I'm guessing this is a pretty big challenge - as a
general tool.

This is not detracting from Flexus FORM PRINT, which many have said along with
SP2 is excellent, (you shouldn't have torn that Money Order up, Bob <G>), nor
have you indicated which compiler you are using, but if POWERCOBOL or Net
Express then you are in luck with OLE support.

I've just finished reading a white paper put together by Wayne Rippin, formerly
M/F but now an independent consultant. (I kid you not, I was reading it only
five minutes prior to accessing your message) :-

    http://www.microfocus.com/files/whitepapers/wpautomation.pdf

The beauty of the whole thing, (Wayne's paper), and I can't enthuse enough, is
that he gives an intro to OLE using COBOLese wording. In essence his article is
invoking the various M/S Office suite products with OLE - in my particular case,
I got a glint in the eye when it came to using Word as a print substitute for
PC_PRINT.

For me, another added bonus, and something ticking away in the back of my mind
for years, graphics, for which I foresaw I would eventually have to buy an
add-on - not so using OLE techniques I can invoke Excel to produce its various
forms of charts.

Now I mentioned the OO word "invoke" which wont turn you on if you are not the
least bit familiar with or not currently using OO. However, subject to a
compiler supporting OO calls (currently limited to F/J or M/F - apologies if
there are others that support this), you can write standard procedural programs
which just invoke the OO classes/methods as necessary, providing you have the
various data types required with AUTOMATION.

Now as to a 'thinking aloud' example, and currently I don't write any software
associated with dollars and cents, ( mine is tied into oil/gas plants), but we
will stick with that QuckBooks Balance Sheet. Note - when I say 'do this' below,
I'm not suggesting manually, but via a COBOL program :-

- Ages ago I tried some simple template/merging in WordPerfect so I know Word
definitely has the same features

- You could create yourself a template file Balance Sheet specifying layout,
fonts etc.
Copy your blank template to a new Word document.

- Assuming a procedural program, (my particular preference would be turn Wayne's
examples into a supporting class and send parameters), either from COBOL
files/DB Tables you can invoke Word passing parameters as an array of values to
be displayed. (I haven't tired it, but you have the option of 'watching' it or
doing it in background mode based on a SendDispatch signal, at least within Net
Express).

- You display Balance Sheet, depending upon number of account entries, user may
have to scroll through 1 to 3 pages.- this is a standard Word scroll. Using the
Quickbooks technique, user wants to view a different reporting period. Merely
copy the master template to a fresh Word document and iterate through above
routine.

How effective - I don't know, but I'm guessing probably 'pretty damned good'. It
*is* worth the research.

Let me stress again, the article is specifically M/F based, but generalized
enough to describe the technique. Any compiler with OLE support must have
parallel features. If it turns out you are using POWERCOBOL, then you can
probably anticipate some extra guidance from other F/J users. Again assuming
your were using F/J, then in all probability they have some decent on-line help
material as well.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: Windows printing from COBOL

- **From:** rtwolfe@flexus.com (Bob Wolfe)
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T13:14:54+00:00

"Newsgroups" <thepla@mail.com> wrote:

>I currently use FORMPRINT and have been successful in creating reports. The
>problem is that FORMPRINT generates one page at a time for viewing and the
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Nesgroups:

I realize that this doesn't directly solve your problem and that the
ideal situation is displaying multiple pages in a scrolling window
format.

But I just wanted to point out that clicking on a button in print
preview isn't the only method for displaying next/previous form in
preview.

It is possible to press a keyboard key combination (I believe that it
is CTRL-PGUP and CTRL-PGDN) to view the prior or next form in preview.
I know it's not a solution, but I personally prefer using keyboard
keys to navigate instead of the mouse.  Hopefully that will make it a
bit easier.





Bob Wolfe
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
When replying by e-mail, make sure that you correct the e-mail address.
Check out The Flexus COBOL Page at http://www.flexus.com
```

```
We've been looking into pdfFactory. It is software that acts like a
printer, but allows print preview.  After loading the software, you
set this printer as your default.  It will then bring up the report in
a print preview mode.

Brad Stark
```

```
Brad Stark <brad@profitsystems.net> wrote in message
news:2255982.0204291339.3a61ed9c@posting.google.com...
> We've been looking into pdfFactory. It is software that acts like a
> printer, but allows print preview.  After loading the software, you
> set this printer as your default.  It will then bring up the report in
> a print preview mode.

FWIW, Don Dickinson has a $25.00 shareware  product called "ddoc," a
programming library which can handle both print and preview, you can set
default printer, get lists of printers, etc, etc, etc..

While it is written for Wndows/BASIC programmers, it wouldn't take too much
to convert the header files for use with COBOL programs.

(And at twenty-five bucks it's certainly affordable!)

More info and trial version online at  dickinson.basicguru.com

MCM
(ddoc user)
```

<a id="t-00c1313c12"></a>
## Thread: problems with tbdisplay in a rexx after calling a cobol program — 2 msgs

### problems with tbdisplay in a rexx after calling a cobol program

- **From:** Michael.Radakovic@commerzbank.com (Michael Radakovic)
- **Date:** 2002-04-28T23:59:37-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<afe7e9.0204282259.2aebb6a2@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello,

I have a problem making a tbdisplay in a rexx zdspdvtb after calling a
clist zd087z from the same rexx.
zd087 is a cobol program.
After returning in the base rexx I make a tbtop tbskip and tbdisplay.
Tbdisplay causes an error message (system abend code 0C4, reason code
00000017)
Can you give me a suggestion how I can solve that kind of problem. 

Thank's a lot 

Michael Radakovic


/* REXX */
/*zdspdvtb
  Einstieg
  Anzeige DV-Anwendungen zur Default-Bearbeitung
TRACE ?I          */
 parse arg ddstatus
 /* TEST!*/
  ADDRESS ISPEXEC "LIBDEF ISPTLIB DATASET ID('DBWE.VERL.ISPTLIB')";
  ADDRESS ISPEXEC "LIBDEF ISPTABL DATASET ID('DBWE.VERL.ISPTLIB')";
  ADDRESS ISPEXEC "LIBDEF ISPPLIB DATASET ID" ,
                            "('DBP3.DD.TXCODE2.ISPPLIB')"

  x =uenv(DB2)
  if x ^= 'OK' then do
    /* schwerer Fehler */
    address ispexec "addpop"
    address ispexec "display panel(zdb2err)"
    address ispexec "rempop"
    exit 12
  end

 address ispexec
 "tbcreate zdvtb keys(dvanw) ",
  "names(dvanwtit) write replace"
 "tbsort zdvtb fields(dvanw)"

 call get_dvanw

 /* tabelle#anz beginn */
 "tbtop zdvtb"
 "tbdispl zdvtb panel(zdvtb) ",
                 "autosel(no) position(rdvtb) ",
                 "cursor(dvsel)"
 tab#rc = rc
 do while tab#rc < 8
    /* command */
    parse upper var dvcmd cmd param .

    if cmd = 'L' then do
      /* eintrag lokalisieren */
      dvanw = param
      "tbscan zdvtb",
       " arglist(dvanw) condlist(ge)",
       " position(rdvtb)"
      ztdtop = rdvtb
    end
    else do
      do while  ztdsels >= 1
        "control display save"

          say 'vor'
          say ddstatus
          say dvanw
          say dvsel
        /* linecommand */
      if dvsel = 'I' then do
           /* anzeigen */
          "vput (ddstatus) shared"
          "vput (dvanw) shared"

           call ztxdvd
        end

        if dvsel = 'D' then do
          /* anzeigen */
          "vput (ddstatus) shared"
          "vput (dvanw) shared"

          say ddstatus
          say dvanw
          say dvsel
          say dvsel
     /*   TRACE ?I  */

           address ispexec "vget (db2syst) shared"
           say db2syst
           db2syst = 'DB2T'
           say db2syst
     /*    queue "DSN SYS("!!db2syst!!") " */
     /*    CALL ZD087Z                     */
           ADDRESS ISPEXEC
          "TBCLOSE zdvtb"
           ADDRESS TSO
          "EXEC 'DBP3.DD.TXCODE2.ISPCLIB(ZD087Z)'"
     /*    queue "DSN SYS("!!DB2T!!") "    */
     /*    queue "RUN PROGRAM(ZD085) "     */
     /*    queue " RUN PROGRAM(ZD087) LIB('SYS4.TEST.PGMLIB') " */
     /*    queue "END"                     */
 /*        ADDRESS ISPEXEC                                          */
 /*            PUSH "END"                                           */
 /*            PUSH "RUN PROGRAM(ZD087) LIB('SYS4.TEST.PGMLIB') "   */
 /*        ADDRESS TSO                                              */
 /*            "DSN SYSTEM(DB2T)"                                   */
 /*             ZISPFRC = RC                                        */
  /*       ADDRESS ISPEXEC  "vget (db2syst) shared"             */
        end
      ADDRESS ISPEXEC
        "control display restore"
        dvsel = ' '
        if ztdsels > 1 then do
           say 'stelle1'
          "tbdispl zdvtb",
           "position(rdvtb) cursor(dvsel)"
          tab#rc = rc
        end
        else do
          ztdsels = 0
        end
      end
    end

    trace ?r
  ADDRESS ISPEXEC "LIBDEF ISPPLIB DATASET ID" ,
                            "('DBP3.DD.TXCODE2.ISPPLIB')"
  ADDRESS ISPEXEC "LIBDEF ISPTLIB DATASET ID('DBWE.VERL.ISPTLIB')";
  ADDRESS ISPEXEC "LIBDEF ISPTABL DATASET ID('DBWE.VERL.ISPTLIB')";
    ADDRESS ISPEXEC
 /*  say queued()   */
     "TBOPEN  zdvtb"
    "tbtop zdvtb"
    say 'stelle3'
    "tbskip zdvtb",
      " noread number("ztdtop")";
      ADDRESS ISPEXEC
    "tbdispl zdvtb panel(zdvtb) ",
      "autosel(no) position(rdvtb) ",
      "csrrow("value(rdvtb)") cursor(dvsel)"
     tab#rc = rc
 end

exit

get_dvanw: procedure

  /*
  x =uenv(DB2)
  if x ^= 'OK' then do
    /* schwerer Fehler */
    address ispexec "addpop"
    address ispexec "display panel(zdb2err)"
    address ispexec "rempop"
    exit 12
  end
  */
  address ispexec "vget (db2syst) shared"
  address db2 "SIGNON "!!db2syst

  address db2
  "DECLARE RXCSR2 FOR ",
  "SELECT A.DVANW, A.DVANWTIT ",
    "FROM CB.ZZDVAWVI A ",
    "WHERE A.ANWSYSKZ = 'ANW'",
    "AND A.OPSYSTKL = '-390'"
  "OPEN RXCSR2"
  if sqlcode = 0 then do
    "FETCH RXCSR2"
    do while sqlcode = 0
      address ispexec "tbadd zdvtb order"
      "FETCH RXCSR2"
    end
    "CLOSE RXCSR2"
  end

  address db2 "SIGNOFF SYNC"

return


PROC 0
 CONTROL MSG NOFLUSH NOPROMPT NOLIST NOSYMLIST
/*ALLOC DD(ZDDCMDO1) DS(TEST.WRITE) OLD  */
 ISPEXEC LIBDEF ISPPLIB  DATASET                               +
                         ID('DBIH.ISPPLIB')
 IF &LASTCC > 0 THEN                                                +
   WRITE LIBDEF ISPPLIB ENDETE MIT &LASTCC

 ISPEXEC LIBDEF ISPMLIB  DATASET                               +
                         ID('DBIH.ISPMLIB')
 IF  &SYSDSN(ZDPV.COMMANDS) = OK THEN +
        DELETE (ZDPV.COMMANDS)
 ALLOC  DD(ZDDCMDO1) DS(ZDPV.COMMANDS)  NEW CATALOG +
                      UNIT(TSOVOL) SPACE(50 30) TRACKS +
                      LRECL(80) BLKSIZE(6400) RECFM(F B) REUSE
 IF  &SYSDSN(ZDPV.DELETEDS) = OK THEN +
        DELETE (ZDPV.DELETEDS)
 ALLOC  DD(ZDDCMDO2) DS(ZDPV.DELETEDS)  NEW CATALOG +
                      UNIT(TSOVOL) SPACE(50 30) TRACKS +
                      LRECL(180) BLKSIZE(14400) RECFM(F B) REUSE
/*ZDLISTA */
  DATA
   DSN SYSTEM(DB2T)                                                   
 00190000
   RUN  PROGRAM(ZD087) LIBRARY('SYS4.TEST.PGMLIB')                    
 00200003
 END                                                                  
 00220000
  ENDDATA
EXIT
```

#### ↳ Re: problems with tbdisplay in a rexx after calling a cobol program

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T08:10:31-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<pmrscuomqlmv4qm5nljrv759qbae2ip580@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<afe7e9.0204282259.2aebb6a2@posting.google.com>`

```
On 28 Apr 2002 23:59:37 -0700 Michael.Radakovic@commerzbank.com (Michael
Radakovic) wrote:

:>I have a problem making a tbdisplay in a rexx zdspdvtb after calling a
:>clist zd087z from the same rexx.
:>zd087 is a cobol program.
:>After returning in the base rexx I make a tbtop tbskip and tbdisplay.
:>Tbdisplay causes an error message (system abend code 0C4, reason code
:>00000017)
:>Can you give me a suggestion how I can solve that kind of problem. 

That is a typical abend for an overlay or freed storage.

By using ADDRESS TSO to invoke the clist and DB2 you are causing the same REXX
function pool to be used. When these programs end the storage used is gone.

Try using 
         ADDRESS ISPEXEC 
         "SELECT CMD(EXEC 'DBP3.DD.TXCODE2.ISPCLIB(ZD087Z)')"
to invoke the CLIST and see if the problem goes away.
```

## Thread: accept number — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

### accept number

- **From:** "Tomba" <tombatore@hotmail.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T22:41:56+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ccdb018$0$8155$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
what little code should I use to accept a 1 or 2 on a screen with just text
on it?
I have this: (please do not mind typing errors .. compiler does not give any
errors, but I'm typing it over)

working-storage section.
01 flag   value 1 pic 9(2) comp-x.
01 key-status.
  04 toets.
  88 cijfer1-key value x"3331".
  88 cijfer2-key value x"3332".
* ...
procedure division.
    call "cbl_set_csr_pos" using cursor-off-screen
    perform test after until cijfer1-key or cijfer2-key
        display g-period
        call x"af" using flag key-status
        accept g-period
        perform verwerk
    end-perform
    stop run

verwerk.
    call x"af" using falg key-status
    evaluate true
        when cijfer1-key move 1 to ws-period
        when cijfer2-key move 2 to ws-period
    end-evaluate.

there must be somthing wrong though with the hex numbers of both
cijferX-key, because in anim it never gets the evaluate to when cijfer1-key,
although I pressed the 1 on keyb

anyone?

greets,
  tomba
```

#### ↳ Re: accept number

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T00:54:37+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CCDEABC.DF762678@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<3ccdb018$0$8155$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
Tomba,

Confusion reigns in your mind. Let's see if we can clarify it for you.

There are three elements, and the programs I already sent you from the M/F
examples, cover #2 and #3 below. Plus further programs show you how to
manipulate colours etc. If you look at the various examples, whilst not
necessarily related to accept/display, they give you some tips on screen layout.

1. Screen layout - which allows you to (a) display myScreen or display this
field, or
    (b) accept MyScreen or accept this field - you are dealing with numerics or
text.

2. The keyboard routines testing for characters or function keys.

3. The Mouse routines for checking where the Mouse is or even placing the Mouse.

You are obviously a beginner, and appear to be trying to do it all in one BIG
BANG. You wont succeed. Do it in order by the numbers above. Get a handle on
simple display screen or accept screen before you move on to #2, then #3.

Now you've already been through #2 where you were testing for Function keys -
but it is in no way the same as # 1.

#1 Screen Section.
-------------------

Keep it simple. Don't use files, just a record in Working Storage.

01 ws-record.
    05 ws-Name            pic x(30).
    05 ws-Balance         pic 9(06)v9(02).

Screen Section.

01 AcceptScreen.
    05 line 6 col 10  value "Customer Name".
    05 line 6 col 30 A-name pic x(30) to ws-name.
    05 line 8 col 10  value "Balance".
    05 line 8 col 30 A-balance pic zzz,zzz.99 to ws-balance..

01 DisplayScreen.
    05 line 6 col 10  value "Customer Name".
    05 line 6 col 30 D-name pic x(30) from ws-name.
    05 line 8 col 10  value "Balance".
    05 line 8 col 30 D-balance pic zzz,zzz.99 from ws-balance..

Procedure Division.

initialize ws-record
move "Joe Blow" to ws-name
move 123.45" to ws-balance
display DisplayScreen

initialize ws-record
accept AcceptScreen

now accept a new balance for the record we have just accepted
accept D-balance

NOW - it is such a long time since I used Screens that the above may not be
totally correct. I'm sure you must be able to use one screen that both accepts
and displays. (one screen line indicating both "To" and "From".  You will just
have to look at your language references and try it out. Note particularly,
don't confuse accepting/displaying with testing for Hex values.

When you have mastered Steps 1, 2 and 3, individually - NOW - you can put them
all together in one program.

Jimmy, Calgary AB

Tomba wrote:

> what little code should I use to accept a 1 or 2 on a screen with just text
> on it?
…[34 more quoted lines elided]…
>   tomba
```

<a id="t-e95f943799"></a>
## Thread: OS/2 and PERCobol — 1 msgs

### OS/2 and PERCobol

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-29T17:24:47-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aakh7l$cpo$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Just to clarify a couple of previous posts on PERCobol development vs
run-time support for OS/2, I got the following note in reply to my inquiry
to the vendor,

"Bill,
PERCobol supports OS/2 as a development platform with PERCobol 2.7.  The
latest release of PERCobol  3.0 has added a new IDE built by LegacyJ rom IBM
Eclipse technology.This new IDE contains language sensitive editing (old one
did as well), incremental compiles, "local" and "global" compile parameters
for mixed COBOL source (important since PERCobol support 15 vendor dialects
of COBOL), and many other nice features.  HOWEVER, PERCobol 3.0 does not
currently support OS/2 as a development platform; the reason is that IBM
does not support OS/2 with Ecplise.  PERCobol 2.7 can still be ordered for
OS/2 but, in the future, we would recommend compiling on an other platform
for deployment to OS/2 that will remain possible for as long as the platform
is supported by IBM.

PERCobol is the COBOL compiler that compiles to Java byte code.  This is
extremely important for those who would like to deploy applications on J2EE
compliant platforms; meaning that PERCobol is the only COBOL compiler that
can generate J2EE class files that are native ONLY to the Java platform. A
wrappering technology that takes a OS native object and wrappers it to
permit it to interface with Java has only created a liability not a
solution. PERCobol  is the only solution available that can compile COBOL
and generate platform independent applications, Servlets and Enterprise
JavaBeans for deployment on Application Servers for Web Services."
```

```
IBM Mainframe converting VS cobol to Cobol 390.
This program has display upon console and accept commands
to verify alignment and beginning and ending check numbers
for printing checks.

Under the old version those messages were highlighted on the console.
Under cobol 390 they no longer highlight and thus scroll away.

The systems programmer will intercept them based on message text
so they don't scroll but it seems to me if I didn't change the
message text, they should behave the same under Cobol 390 as
they do under VS cobol.  Any ideas?  Thanks.

<a id="t-42959f1d03"></a>
## Thread: OO COBOL and IBM — 3 this month / 5 total

_Topics: [`Object-oriented COBOL`](topics.md#oo)_

### OO COBOL and IBM

- **From:** "Ahangar" <eahangar@newsguy.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T09:48:30-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aam76r014ao@enews1.newsguy.com>`

```
Has IBM announced their position concerning support of proposed OO COBOL?  I
tried to search the IBM site and did not find anything specific.



Thanks
```

#### ↳ Re: OO COBOL and IBM

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T14:49:46-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aamsh1$15t$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<aam76r014ao@enews1.newsguy.com>`

```
IBM *does* currently support OO COBOL - but *not* exactly what is in the
draft ANSI/ISO Standard.  (The OO support in Enterprise COBOL, the latest
compiler, is NOT the same as what was in IBM COBOL for OS/390 & VM, the last
compiler).

IBM has made NO public statements concerning implementing any, all, none of
the draft Standard.  My guess is that they will support at least SOME of it,
but that is only a guess.

P.S.  This answer assumes an "OS/390 mainframe" interest.  IBM also provides
OO support on WinTel and AIX - but does not on VM, VSE, OS/400
```

#### ↳ Re: OO COBOL and IBM

- **From:** stephenjspiro@mail.com (Stephen J Spiro)
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T19:06:15-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<928495c6.0204301806.67c120f4@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<aam76r014ao@enews1.newsguy.com>`

```
"Ahangar" <eahangar@newsguy.com> wrote in message news:<aam76r014ao@enews1.newsguy.com>...
> Has IBM announced their position concerning support of proposed OO COBOL?  I
> tried to search the IBM site and did not find anything specific.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Thanks

The new "Enterprise COBOL" looks great! LOTS of new stuff!  They have
finally switched from the "old" OO syntax to the "New (after 1996)"
syntax, but not completely.  This is not IBM's fault -- J4 was still
ironing out the details a few months ago.  IBM went with what was done
when they wrote the compiler.  I expect (Professional OPINION) that
they will update it with the next release.


This compiler should put to rest any fears that IBM is letting COBOL
slide... (again, professional OPINION).

Stephen J Spiro
J4 COBOL Standards Committee
```

<a id="t-514a4ec147"></a>
## Thread: .dat and .idx file conversion — 4 this month / 6 total

### .dat and .idx file conversion

- **From:** mike@dsacentral.com (Mike)
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T09:03:55-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9e862e20.0204300803.f652244@posting.google.com>`

```
We are reviewing an old application for a client.  Our assumption is
that it was written in MicroFocus Cobol and uses .dat and .idx
datafiles.  Is there a utility available that can show us the
structure of these files.  We have no source code and the developer no
longer walks the earth.  Any help would be appreciated.

Murf
```

#### ↳ Re: .dat and .idx file conversion

- **From:** Ed Guy <ed_guy@paralynx.com>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T10:02:05-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CCECE0D.7AF6@paralynx.com>`
- **References:** `<9e862e20.0204300803.f652244@posting.google.com>`

```
Mike wrote:
> 
> We are reviewing an old application for a client.  Our assumption is
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> longer walks the earth.  Any help would be appreciated.

It's not easy.  For the DAT files something like ParseRat (http://www.parserat.com/) 
will help in the trial and error process (use the "binary" settings) - you can usually 
see patterns which help you establish record lengths and header size (if any).  Then it 
will help you identify field sizes the same way and show you what each field would 
contain in various formats (e.g. packed, binary, etc).  Once you've got that done, it 
will extract the data to any one of a variety of "importable" formats for other 
packages.

You'll need SOME idea of what  are valid values for numeric data.

IDX files will be indices and you can re-generate them once you have the data extracted 
into some other system.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: .dat and .idx file conversion

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-04-30T19:05:58+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3CCEEAB2.10ACF88C@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<9e862e20.0204300803.f652244@posting.google.com> <3CCECE0D.7AF6@paralynx.com>`

```


Ed Guy wrote:

> Mike wrote:
> >
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Just as an add-on to what Ed has written - and really your only hope - do you have any
source programs, from the deceased programer, that reference the files you are talking
about. Best situation if he has used copyfiles that you can get your hands on.

Again referring to source, but with no copyfiles - you are talking indexed (ISAMs) -
probably many will be 'Master Files' that need editing. Do the edit programs show you which
fields are being edited -still a tough option, but you are slightly ahead.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

```

large .txt data base of customers in a cd rom.  Right now im having a
problem extracting them and reading them indevidually just to see to
sample of it.

All I want to do with the data is get the first names, last names the
name of the bussiness and how much they owe. And i want to be able to
use thier fisr name, last name or thier busseness to find out how much
they owe.  I just want to know whats the best way of extracting this
info individually, or extraxting them in groups of 10 or 20. I know
its a simple code to write b/c there is no arithmatic or anything
involved. I havent programed anything in solong I Just dont know where
to start.  I wold like to know what program i should use and  how to
begin on wrting the code.  If there
is a software that could do this let me know? A software wont be as
fun but it sure will be easier but hey all suggestions are are
welcome.


TIA
Ambesa
